269	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
270		if w.Store == nil {
271			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
272		}
273		if w.Executor == nil {
274			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
275		}
276	
277		now := w.Now
278		if now == nil {
279			now = time.Now
280		}
281		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
282		if assignee == "" {
283			assignee = "ddx"
284		}
285		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
286		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
287			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
288		}
289		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
290		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
291			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
292		}
293		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
294		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
295			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
296		}
297		harness := rcfg.Harness()
298		model := rcfg.Model()
299		profile := rcfg.Profile()
300	
301		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
302		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
303	
304		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
305			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
306		}
307	
308		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
309			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
310			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
311			"harness":      harness,
312			"model":        model,
313			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
314			"assignee":     assignee,
315			"once":         runtime.Once,
316		})
317		defer func() {
318			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
319				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
320				"successes":           result.Successes,
321				"failures":            result.Failures,
322				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
323			})
324		}()
325	
326		for {
327			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
328			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
329			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
330			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
331			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
332				return result, err
333			}
334	
335			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, runtime.LabelFilter)
336			if err != nil {
337				return result, err
338			}
339			if !ok {
340				if result.Attempts == 0 {
341					result.NoReadyWork = true
342					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
343						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
344							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
345								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
346								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
347								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
348								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
349								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
350							}
351						}
352					}
353				}
354				if runtime.PollInterval <= 0 {
355					return result, nil
356				}
357				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, runtime.PollInterval); err != nil {
358					return result, err
359				}
360				continue
361			}
362	
363			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
364	
365			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
366			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
367			// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
368			if runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {
369				if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
370					if runtime.Log != nil {
371						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
372					}
373					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
374						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
375						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
376					})
377					continue
378				}
379			}
380	
381			// Routing preflight gate (FEAT-006 D3, ddx-98e6e9ef): consult the
382			// upstream typed-incompatibility surface BEFORE claiming. If the
383			// configured (harness, model) cannot serve the bead, exit the loop
384			// with a worker-level failure record — no claim, no executor
385			// invocation, no tier-attempt event burn.
386			if runtime.RoutePreflight != nil {
387				if rerr := runtime.RoutePreflight(ctx, harness, model); rerr != nil {
388					detail := fmt.Sprintf("routing preflight rejected (harness=%s model=%s): %s",
389						harness, model, rerr.Error())
390					if runtime.Log != nil {
391						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "routing preflight: %s (skipping %s, exiting loop)\n",
392							detail, candidate.ID)
393					}
394					emit("preflight.rejected", map[string]any{
395						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
396						"harness": harness,
397						"model":   model,
398						"reason":  rerr.Error(),
399					})
400					report := ExecuteBeadReport{
401						BeadID:  candidate.ID,
402						Status:  ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
403						Detail:  detail,
404						Harness: harness,
405						Model:   model,
406					}
407					result.Attempts++
408					result.Failures++
409					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
410					result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
411					return result, nil
412				}
413			}
414	
415			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
416				continue
417			}
418	
419			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
420				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
421				"title":    candidate.Title,
422				"assignee": assignee,
423			})
424	
425			if runtime.Log != nil {
426				if candidate.Title != "" {
427					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
428				} else {
429					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
430				}
431			}
432	
433			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
434			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
435			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
436			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
437			runStart := now()
438			phaseSeq := 0
439			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
440				phaseSeq++
441				emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
442					runtime.WorkerID, runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
443					harness, model, profile,
444					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
445				))
446			}
447	
448			nextPhase("queueing", false)
449	
450			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
451			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
452			hbWG.Add(1)
453			go func(beadID string) {
454				defer hbWG.Done()
455				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
456				defer ticker.Stop()
457				for {
458					select {
459					case <-hbCtx.Done():
460						return
461					case <-ticker.C:
462						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)
463					}
464				}
465			}(candidate.ID)
466	
467			nextPhase("running", false)
468	
469			report, err := w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)
470			hbCancel()
471			hbWG.Wait()
472			if err != nil {
473				report = ExecuteBeadReport{
474					BeadID: candidate.ID,
475					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
476					Detail: err.Error(),
477				}
478			}
479			if report.BeadID == "" {
480				report.BeadID = candidate.ID
481			}
482			if report.Status == "" {
483				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
484			}
485			if report.Detail == "" {
486				report.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(report.Status, "", "")
487			}
488	
489			result.Attempts++
490	
491			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
492				appendLoopRoutingEvidence(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, now().UTC())
493				// Close the bead early when review is skipped. The closure gate
494				// (ddx-e30e60a9) accepts this path because closing_commit_sha is
495				// set and there is no malformed-APPROVE event to reject.
496				reviewApproved := true
497				reviewSkipped := w.Reviewer == nil || runtime.NoReview || HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip")
498	
499				if reviewSkipped {
500					if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
501						return result, err
502					}
503				}
504	
505				if w.Reviewer != nil && !runtime.NoReview && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip") {
506					reviewRes, reviewErr := w.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev, report.Harness, report.Model)
507					if reviewErr != nil {
508						// FEAT-022 §12+§14: classify the failure into the four-class
509						// taxonomy and count prior review-error events scoped to the
510						// current result_rev. On the Nth failure emit a terminal
511						// review-manual-required event instead of yet another
512						// review-error, parking the bead so a subsequent loop
513						// iteration does NOT re-execute primary.
514						class := classifyReviewError(reviewErr, reviewRes)
515						prior := countPriorReviewErrors(w.Store, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev)
516						attemptCount := prior + 1
517						maxRetries := rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries()
518						if maxRetries <= 0 {
519							maxRetries = DefaultReviewMaxRetries
520						}
521						errSummary := EventBodySummary{
522							InputBytes:  0,
523							OutputBytes: 0,
524						}
525						if reviewRes != nil {
526							errSummary = EventBodySummary{
527								Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
528								Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
529								InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
530								OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
531								ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
532							}
533						}
534						if attemptCount >= maxRetries {
535							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
536							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
537								Kind:      "review-manual-required",
538								Summary:   class,
539								Body:      body,
540								Actor:     assignee,
541								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
542								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
543							})
544							// Park the bead with a long cooldown so subsequent
545							// loop iterations do NOT re-pick it for primary
546							// re-execution. Reviewer-failure invariant (§13) is
547							// preserved: the bead is NOT closed.
548							parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))
549							_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, "review-manual-required", class)
550						} else {
551							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
552							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
553								Kind:      "review-error",
554								Summary:   class,
555								Body:      body,
556								Actor:     assignee,
557								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
558								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
559							})
560						}
561					} else {
562						report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
563						report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
564						// Persist the full reviewer stream as an artifact so the
565						// event body never carries the raw stream (ddx-f8a11202).
566						// On error the artifact path is empty and the event body
567						// still contains the short verdict summary; callers of this
568						// loop can recover the full text from the reviewer session
569						// log if the artifact write failed.
570						artifactPath, artifactErr := persistReviewerStream(runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, report.AttemptID, reviewRes.RawOutput)
571						if artifactErr != nil && runtime.Log != nil {
572							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "reviewer stream artifact: %v\n", artifactErr)
573						}
574	
575						reviewSummary := EventBodySummary{
576							Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
577							Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
578							InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
579							OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
580							ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
581						}
582						switch reviewRes.Verdict {
583						case VerdictApprove:
584							// Approved: record the verdict event and then close.
585							// Closure must land AFTER the review event so the
586							// gate (ddx-e30e60a9) sees the terminal verdict.
587							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
588								Kind:      "review",
589								Summary:   "APPROVE",
590								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("APPROVE", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
591								Actor:     assignee,
592								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
593								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
594							})
595							if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); cerr != nil {
596								return result, cerr
597							}
598						case VerdictRequestChanges:
599							// Needs fixes: record the review verdict, then reopen
600							// with findings in notes. The review event must land
601							// even on non-approve paths so review-outcomes can
602							// attribute the rejection to the originating tier.
603							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
604								Kind:      "review",
605								Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
606								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
607								Actor:     assignee,
608								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
609								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
610							})
611							reopenNotes := reviewRes.Rationale
612							if reopenNotes == "" {
613								reopenNotes = reviewRes.RawOutput
614							}
615							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: REQUEST_CHANGES", reopenNotes); reopenErr != nil {
616								return result, reopenErr
617							}
618							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
619							report.Detail = "post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
620							reviewApproved = false
621						case VerdictBlock:
622							rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
623							if rationale == "" {
624								_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
625									Kind:      "review-malfunction",
626									Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
627									Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath), reviewSummary),
628									Actor:     assignee,
629									Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
630									CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
631								})
632								report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
633								report.Detail = "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
634								report.ReviewRationale = ""
635								reviewApproved = false
636								break
637							}
638							// Cannot proceed: record the verdict, then reopen and
639							// flag for human with BLOCK marker plus actionable rationale.
640							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
641								Kind:      "review",
642								Summary:   "BLOCK",
643								Body:      appendEventSummary(rationale, reviewSummary),
644								Actor:     assignee,
645								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
646								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
647							})
648							blockNotes := "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n" + rationale
649							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: BLOCK", blockNotes); reopenErr != nil {
650								return result, reopenErr
651							}
652							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
653							report.Detail = "post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)"
654							reviewApproved = false
655						}
656					}
657				}
658	
659				if reviewApproved {
660					result.Successes++
661					result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
662				} else {
663					result.Failures++
664					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
665				}
666			} else {
667				if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
668					return result, err
669				}
670				if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges {
671					count, cerr := w.Store.IncrNoChangesCount(candidate.ID)
672					if cerr != nil {
673						return result, cerr
674					}
675					satisfied, evidence, aerr := w.adjudicateNoChanges(ctx, candidate.ID, count, maxNoChangesBeforeClose, report.NoChangesRationale, candidate.Acceptance, runtime.ProjectRoot)
676					if aerr != nil {
677						return result, aerr
678					}
679					if satisfied {
680						// Adjudication confirmed bead is already satisfied.
681						// Set the terminal status BEFORE the close so the late
682						// executeBeadLoopEvent append captures "already_satisfied"
683						// (not "no_changes"), and emit an early execute-bead
684						// evidence event so the closure gate accepts even when
685						// BaseRev is empty (test fixtures and genuinely-no-commit
686						// satisfied beads).
687						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
688						if evidence != "" {
689							// Checker evidence explains why the bead is being closed;
690							// it takes precedence over the executor's attempt detail.
691							report.Detail = evidence
692						}
693						_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC()))
694						if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.BaseRev); cerr != nil {
695							return result, cerr
696						}
697						result.Successes++
698						result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
699					} else {
700						// Unresolved: suppress immediate retry so the queue can
701						// move on to other beads.
702						if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
703							retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(noProgressCooldown))
704							if cerr := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); cerr != nil {
705								return result, cerr
706							}
707							report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
708						}
709						result.Failures++
710						result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
711					}
712				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition {
713					// Executor declined the bead because it is too large to
714					// deliver in one pass. Record a structured
715					// `decomposition-recommendation` event and park the bead
716					// long enough that subsequent loop iterations do NOT
717					// re-attempt it. An operator (or helix-evolve) clears the
718					// cooldown — typically by splitting the bead.
719					body, mErr := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
720						"rationale":            report.DecompositionRationale,
721						"recommended_subbeads": report.DecompositionRecommendation,
722						"detail":               report.Detail,
723						"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
724						"session_id":           report.SessionID,
725					})
726					bodyStr := ""
727					if mErr == nil {
728						bodyStr = string(body)
729					} else {
730						bodyStr = fmt.Sprintf("rationale=%s\nrecommended_subbeads=%v",
731							report.DecompositionRationale, report.DecompositionRecommendation)
732					}
733					summary := "agent declined: needs decomposition"
734					if n := len(report.DecompositionRecommendation); n > 0 {
735						summary = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%d sub-beads)", summary, n)
736					}
737					_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
738						Kind:      "decomposition-recommendation",
739						Summary:   summary,
740						Body:      bodyStr,
741						Actor:     assignee,
742						Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
743						CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
744					})
745					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))
746					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
747						return result, err
748					}
749					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
750					result.Failures++
751					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
752				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed {
753					// Push failed after a successful local merge/ff. The bead is
754					// NOT closed (commits live only locally) — park it under the
755					// 24h cap so the loop re-attempts within a reasonable horizon.
756					// The push stderr in report.Detail is recorded as last_detail
757					// so it surfaces on the bead and on any direct claim attempt.
758					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))
759					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
760						return result, err
761					}
762					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
763					result.Failures++
764					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
765				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict {
766					// The local target ref was advanced and the loop's
767					// auto-pull/merge/retry recovery hit a merge conflict it
768					// cannot resolve without operator input. Record a
769					// `push-conflict` event with the conflict context so the
770					// operator can find and resolve it, and park the bead under
771					// the same 24h cap as push_failed.
772					body, mErr := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
773						"detail":     report.Detail,
774						"base_rev":   report.BaseRev,
775						"result_rev": report.ResultRev,
776						"session_id": report.SessionID,
777					})
778					bodyStr := report.Detail
779					if mErr == nil {
780						bodyStr = string(body)
781					}
782					_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
783						Kind:      "push-conflict",
784						Summary:   "auto-merge after push race could not be resolved",
785						Body:      bodyStr,
786						Actor:     assignee,
787						Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
788						CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
789					})
790					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))
791					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
792						return result, err
793					}
794					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
795					result.Failures++
796					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
797				} else {
798					if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
799						retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(noProgressCooldown))
800						if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
801							return result, err
802						}
803						report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
804					}
805					result.Failures++
806					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
807				}
808			}
809	
810			result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
811	
812			// Skip the late execute-bead append for already-satisfied beads —
813			// the satisfied path appends its own terminal event before
814			// CloseWithEvidence so the closure gate sees execution evidence.
815			// Duplicating it here would yield two identical events.
816			if report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
817				if err := w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC())); err != nil {
818					return result, err
819				}
820			}
821	
822			// Emit terminal progress phase event.
823			terminalPhase := "failed"
824			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
825				terminalPhase = "done"
826			} else if report.PreserveRef != "" {
827				terminalPhase = "preserved"
828			}
829			// Use the real attempt_id from the report if available.
830			finalAttemptID := report.AttemptID
831			if finalAttemptID == "" {
832				finalAttemptID = provAttemptID
833			}
834			phaseSeq++
835			emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
836				EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
837				AttemptID: finalAttemptID,
838				WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
839				ProjectID: runtime.ProjectRoot,
840				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
841				Harness:   harness,
842				Model:     model,
843				Profile:   profile,
844				Phase:     terminalPhase,
845				PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
846				Heartbeat: false,
847				TS:        now().UTC(),
848				ElapsedMS: now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
849				Message:   report.Detail,
850			})
851	
852			emit("bead.result", map[string]any{
853				"bead_id":              candidate.ID,
854				"status":               report.Status,
855				"detail":               report.Detail,
856				"session_id":           report.SessionID,
857				"result_rev":           report.ResultRev,
858				"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
859				"preserve_ref":         report.PreserveRef,
860				"no_changes_rationale": report.NoChangesRationale,
861				"duration_ms":          now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
862			})
863	
864			if runtime.Log != nil {
865				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "✓ %s → %s\n", candidate.ID, formatLoopResult(report))
866			}
867	
868			if runtime.Once {
869				return result, nil
870			}
871		}
872	}
873	
874	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) nextCandidate(attempted map[string]struct{}, labelFilter string) (bead.Bead, bool, error) {
875		ready, err := w.Store.ReadyExecution()
876		if err != nil {
877			return bead.Bead{}, false, err
878		}
879		for _, candidate := range ready {
880			if _, seen := attempted[candidate.ID]; seen {
881				continue
882			}
883			if labelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, labelFilter) {
884				continue
885			}
886			return candidate, true, nil
887		}
888		return bead.Bead{}, false, nil
889	}
890	
891	// appendLoopRoutingEvidence records a kind:routing evidence event on the bead
892	// from the executor's ExecuteBeadReport, so that review-outcomes analytics can
893	// attribute a subsequent review verdict to the originating provider/model tier.
894	// Best-effort: errors and missing-provider cases are silently ignored.
895	func appendLoopRoutingEvidence(store BeadEventAppender, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport, createdAt time.Time) {
896		if store == nil || beadID == "" {
897			return
898		}
899		provider := report.Provider
900		if provider == "" {
901			provider = report.Harness
902		}
903		if provider == "" {
904			return
905		}
906		body, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
907			"resolved_provider": provider,
908			"resolved_model":    report.Model,
909			"fallback_chain":    []string{},
910			"requested_profile": report.RequestedProfile,
911			"requested_tier":    report.RequestedTier,
912			"resolved_tier":     report.ResolvedTier,
913			"escalation_count":  report.EscalationCount,
914			"final_tier":        report.FinalTier,
915		})
916		if err != nil {
917			return
918		}
919		summary := "provider=" + provider
920		if report.Model != "" {
921			summary += " model=" + report.Model
922		}
923		_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
924			Kind:      "routing",
925			Summary:   summary,
926			Body:      string(body),
927			Actor:     "ddx",
928			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
929			CreatedAt: createdAt,
930		})
931	}
932	
933	func executeBeadLoopEvent(report ExecuteBeadReport, actor string, createdAt time.Time) bead.BeadEvent {
934		parts := []string{}
935		if report.Detail != "" {
936			parts = append(parts, report.Detail)
937		}
938		if report.Tier != "" {
939			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s", report.Tier))
940		}
941		if report.ProbeResult != "" {
942			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("probe_result=%s", report.ProbeResult))
943		}
944		if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
945			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("rationale: %s", report.NoChangesRationale))
946		}
947		if report.ReviewRationale != "" {
948			parts = append(parts, report.ReviewRationale)
949		}
950		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
951			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("preserve_ref=%s", report.PreserveRef))
952		}
953		if report.ResultRev != "" {
954			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("result_rev=%s", report.ResultRev))
955		}
956		if report.BaseRev != "" {
957			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("base_rev=%s", report.BaseRev))
958		}
959		if report.RetryAfter != "" {
960			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("retry_after=%s", report.RetryAfter))
961		}
962	
963		return bead.BeadEvent{
964			Kind:      "execute-bead",
965			Summary:   report.Status,
966			Body:      strings.Join(parts, "\n"),
967			Actor:     actor,
968			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
969			CreatedAt: createdAt,
970		}
971	}
972	
973	// writeLoopEvent emits one structured JSONL line to sink describing a
974	// milestone in an execute-bead loop run. Entries use the same envelope as
975	// the ddx-agent harness (session_id/seq/type/ts/data) so existing log
976	// aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, ddx server workers log) can parse
977	// the stream uniformly. Errors are swallowed: structured logging must
978	// never break the core execute-loop.
979	func writeLoopEvent(sink io.Writer, sessionID, eventType string, data map[string]any, ts time.Time) {
980		if sink == nil {
981			return
982		}
983		if data == nil {
984			data = map[string]any{}
985		}
986		entry := map[string]any{
987			"session_id": sessionID,
988			"type":       eventType,
989			"ts":         ts.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
990			"data":       data,
991		}
992		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
993		if err != nil {
994			return
995		}
996		_, _ = sink.Write(line)
997		_, _ = sink.Write([]byte("\n"))
998	}
999	
1000	// formatLoopResult returns a concise human-readable summary of a bead execution
1001	// result using merged/preserved/error terminology instead of raw status codes.
1002	func formatLoopResult(report ExecuteBeadReport) string {
1003		switch report.Status {
1004		case ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
1005			shortRev := report.ResultRev
1006			if len(shortRev) > 8 {
1007				shortRev = shortRev[:8]
1008			}
1009			if shortRev != "" {
1010				return fmt.Sprintf("merged (%s)", shortRev)
1011			}
1012			return "merged"
1013		case ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
1014			return "already_satisfied"
1015		case ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
1016			if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
1017				return fmt.Sprintf("no_changes: %s", report.NoChangesRationale)
1018			}
1019			return "no_changes"
1020		default:
1021			detail := report.Detail
1022			if detail == "" {
1023				detail = report.Status
1024			}
1025			if report.PreserveRef != "" {
1026				return fmt.Sprintf("preserved: %s", detail)
1027			}
1028			return fmt.Sprintf("error: %s", detail)
1029		}
1030	}
1031	
1032	// reCommitSHA matches a 7-to-40 character lowercase hex string that looks like a
1033	// git commit SHA. Used to detect whether a no_changes rationale cites a prior commit.
1034	var reCommitSHA = regexp.MustCompile(`\b[0-9a-f]{7,40}\b`)
1035	
1036	// reTestFuncName matches a Go test function name (TestXxx or BenchmarkXxx).
1037	var reTestFuncName = regexp.MustCompile(`\b(?:Test|Benchmark)[A-Z]\w*\b`)
1038	
1039	// rationaleIsSpecific returns true when the rationale string contains a reference
1040	// specific enough to treat a no_changes outcome as already_satisfied on the first
1041	// attempt. Currently this means: the rationale cites a commit SHA (7+ hex chars)
1042	// or a Go test function name. Vague rationales ("nothing to do") return false.
1043	func rationaleIsSpecific(rationale string) bool {
1044		if rationale == "" {
1045			return false
1046		}
1047		return reCommitSHA.MatchString(rationale) || reTestFuncName.MatchString(rationale)
1048	}
1049	
1050	// adjudicateNoChanges runs the no-change adjudication step for a bead.
1051	// It returns (satisfied, evidence, err). When satisfied is true the bead
1052	// should be closed as already_satisfied with the evidence string. When false
1053	// retry suppression (cooldown) should be applied and the bead left open.
1054	//
1055	// If a SatisfactionChecker is configured it is called first. Otherwise:
1056	//   - When the report carries a specific rationale (cites a commit SHA or test
1057	//     name), the bead is closed as already_satisfied on the first occurrence.
1058	//   - Otherwise the default count-based rule applies (close after maxNoChangesBeforeClose).
1059	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) adjudicateNoChanges(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount, maxNoChangesBeforeClose int, rationale, acceptance, projectRoot string) (bool, string, error) {
1060		if w.SatisfactionChecker != nil {
1061			return w.SatisfactionChecker.CheckSatisfied(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
1062		}
1063		candidate := false
1064		evidence := ""
1065		switch {
1066		case rationaleIsSpecific(rationale):
1067			candidate = true
1068			evidence = rationale
1069		case noChangesCount >= maxNoChangesBeforeClose:
1070			candidate = true
1071			evidence = fmt.Sprintf("no_changes on %d consecutive attempt(s); bead treated as already satisfied", noChangesCount)
1072		}
1073		if !candidate {
1074			return false, "", nil
1075		}
1076		// Tighten the gate: when AC names structural properties (test functions,
1077		// deleted files, removed struct fields), refuse already_satisfied unless
1078		// each property holds in the worktree / rationale. Prevents false closes
1079		// where a regression suite passes but the AC's specific contract is unmet.
1080		if claims := ParseACClaims(acceptance); len(claims) > 0 {
1081			if ok, why := VerifyACClaims(claims, projectRoot, rationale); !ok {
1082				return false, why, nil
1083			}
1084		}
1085		return true, evidence, nil
1086	}
1087	
1088	func shouldSuppressNoProgress(report ExecuteBeadReport) bool {
1089		if report.BaseRev == "" || report.ResultRev == "" {
1090			return false
1091		}
1092		return report.BaseRev == report.ResultRev
1093	}
1094	
1095	// classifyReviewError maps a reviewer error and partial result into one of the
1096	// four FEAT-022 §12 taxonomy classes. Resolution order:
1097	//  1. reviewRes.Error if it's already one of the canonical class identifiers
1098	//     (the reviewer set it explicitly — preferred path).
1099	//  2. The error string text (for backwards-compatible reviewers that only
1100	//     embed the class in their message).
1101	//  3. ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a fallback when the strict-parse error
1102	//     leaks through a reviewer that did not set Error.
1103	//  4. Default to transport (network/provider-side failure) when nothing else
1104	//     matches.
1105	func classifyReviewError(reviewErr error, reviewRes *ReviewResult) string {
1106		if reviewRes != nil {
1107			switch reviewRes.Error {
1108			case evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1109				evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
1110				evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
1111				evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport:
1112				return reviewRes.Error
1113			}
1114		}
1115		msg := ""
1116		if reviewErr != nil {
1117			msg = reviewErr.Error()
1118		}
1119		switch {
1120		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow):
1121			return evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow
1122		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty):
1123			return evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
1124		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable):
1125			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1126		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport):
1127			return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1128		case reviewErr != nil && strings.Contains(msg, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable.Error()):
1129			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1130		}
1131		return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1132	}
1133	
1134	// reResultRevField extracts the result_rev value from a structured review-error
1135	// event body (one `result_rev=<sha>` line per event, written by
1136	// reviewErrorEventBody). The format is intentionally line-oriented so it
1137	// survives the AppendEvent body cap without losing the rev association.
1138	var reResultRevField = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^result_rev=([^\s]+)\s*$`)
1139	
1140	// countPriorReviewErrors returns the number of `review-error` events already
1141	// recorded against this bead whose body cites the given result_rev. This is
1142	// the FEAT-022 §14 retry counter — it is event-scoped (no separate counter
1143	// field on the bead) so a fresh result_rev naturally resets the count.
1144	func countPriorReviewErrors(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, resultRev string) int {
1145		if resultRev == "" {
1146			return 0
1147		}
1148		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
1149		if err != nil {
1150			return 0
1151		}
1152		n := 0
1153		for _, ev := range events {
1154			if ev.Kind != "review-error" {
1155				continue
1156			}
1157			m := reResultRevField.FindStringSubmatch(ev.Body)
1158			if m == nil {
1159				continue
1160			}
1161			if m[1] == resultRev {
1162				n++
1163			}
1164		}
1165		return n
1166	}
1167	
1168	// reviewErrorEventBody is the canonical body shape for review-error and
1169	// review-manual-required events. It carries the failure class, attempt count,
1170	// and result_rev as discrete lines so operators can grep without parsing the
1171	// full reviewer error text. The trailing free-form message is the raw
1172	// reviewer-error string for forensics.
1173	func reviewErrorEventBody(class string, attemptCount int, resultRev, message string) string {
1174		var b strings.Builder
1175		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "failure_class=%s\n", class)
1176		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "attempt_count=%d\n", attemptCount)
1177		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
1178		if message != "" {
1179			b.WriteString("\n")
1180			b.WriteString(message)
1181		}
1182		return b.String()
1183	}
1184	
1185	func sleepWithContext(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration) error {
1186		if d <= 0 {
1187			return nil
1188		}
1189		timer := time.NewTimer(d)
1190		defer timer.Stop()
1191		select {
1192		case <-ctx.Done():
1193			return ctx.Err()
1194		case <-timer.C:
1195			return nil
1196		}
1197	}
1198

1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to DDx are documented in this file.
4	
5	## [Unreleased]
6	
7	### Breaking changes
8	
9	#### Removed: `agent.routing.default_harness`
10	
11	The `agent.routing.default_harness` field has been removed from `.ddx/config.yaml`
12	(bead ddx-87fb72c2). DDx now refuses to load any config that still carries it
13	and prints a migration pointer instead.
14	
15	Why: `default_harness` was a silent fallback that competed with the top-level
16	`agent.harness` setting and the live route-resolution call graph. It produced
17	confusing routing decisions when the upstream service had no viable provider for
18	the requested profile. The endpoint-first redesign (epic ddx-fdd3ea36) makes
19	harness selection an explicit decision per dispatch — a config-level "default"
20	no longer fits.
21	
22	Migration: delete the field. The top-level `agent.harness` is still honored as
23	a tie-break preference on the resolution path (NOT a default override). See
24	[docs/migrations/routing-config.md](docs/migrations/routing-config.md).
25	
26	#### Opt-in: `agent.routing.profile_ladders` and `agent.routing.model_overrides`
27	
28	These fields are no longer consulted on the default execute path. They are now
29	explicit opt-in:
30	
31	- `profile_ladders` is consulted only when `--escalate` is passed.
32	- `model_overrides` is consulted only when the new `--override-model` flag is
33	  passed.
34	
35	Configs that still set these fields will load successfully but emit a one-time
36	process warning at config-load time. To silence the warning, either pass the
37	opt-in flag on the relevant invocation or remove the field. See the migration
38	guide for details.
39	
40	### Fix: `latency_ms` in claude harness traces now reflects per-call duration
41	
42	Previously every `llm.response` event in `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-claude-*.jsonl`
43	had `latency_ms == elapsed_ms` because the claude stream parser wrote the
44	cumulative wall-clock value into both fields. Per-call LLM latency was
45	therefore unrecoverable from captured traces.
46	
47	The parser now tracks the time the most recent LLM request was sent (session
48	start, then reset on each `user`/tool_result event) and emits
49	`latency_ms = now - lastRequestSentAt` for each assistant turn. `elapsed_ms`
50	remains cumulative, so for any event past turn 1 the two values diverge.
51	
52	Legacy traces produced before this commit have `latency_ms == elapsed_ms` and
53	should be ignored by latency-analysis tooling for runs predating the fix.
54	
55	Regression coverage: `TestParseClaudeStreamLatencyIsPerCall` in
56	`cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go` drives two assistant turns through
57	the parser with real sleeps and asserts (a) `latency_ms != elapsed_ms` for
58	turn 2, (b) the second-turn latency excludes the prior turn and tool window,
59	and (c) the sum of per-call latencies does not exceed the final event's
60	`elapsed_ms`.
61	
62	### execute-loop: recoverable push races and a 24h cap on loop-set cooldowns
63	
64	Bead ddx-a458af7c. Two related fixes:
65	
66	1. **Push-race auto-recovery.** When a worker's commits land locally but `git
67	   push` is rejected because origin advanced (the common multi-worker /
68	   multi-operator case), the land coordinator now attempts one
69	   fetch + merge + retry-push pass before giving up. Previously the bead
70	   was parked for a year and required manual operator intervention even
71	   though the work was already done locally and the merge was trivial. On a
72	   clean auto-merge the land returns with `PushRecovered=true` and the bead
73	   closes normally; the operator never sees the race.
74	
75	2. **`push_conflict` is a distinct, structured outcome.** When the
76	   auto-recovery merge hits a real conflict (overlapping changes that the
77	   loop cannot resolve), Land() reports `PushConflict=true` and the loop
78	   maps it to a new `push_conflict` execute-bead status — distinct from the
79	   generic `push_failed` (and from `execution_failed`). The loop appends a
80	   `kind:push-conflict` bead event carrying the conflict context (detail,
81	   `base_rev`, `result_rev`, `session_id`) so an operator can find and
82	   resolve it without combing logs.
83	
84	3. **Loop-set cooldowns cap at 24h.** Previously several paths
85	   (`push_failed`, `declined_needs_decomposition`, `review-manual-required`)
86	   parked beads for 365 days, which effectively meant "never retry." That
87	   should be a deliberate operator decision via `ddx bead update --set
88	   execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic loop output. A new
89	   constant `MaxLoopCooldown = 24h` and helper `capLoopCooldown` now clamp
90	   every `SetExecutionCooldown` call the loop makes. Operators extending a
91	   cooldown manually beyond 24h still works — the cap only governs
92	   loop-driven cooldowns.
93	
94	   Note that this also changes the `declined_needs_decomposition` cooldown
95	   shipped earlier in this Unreleased section (the 365-day park described
96	   above); under the new cap, decomposition-declined beads re-surface within
97	   24h instead of being parked for a year. Operators clearing the cooldown
98	   via `ddx bead cooldown clear <id>` (the recommended path) is unchanged.
99	
100	Regression coverage:

7	# Feature: DDx Agent Service (consumer of ddx-agent contract)
8	
9	**ID:** FEAT-006
10	**Status:** In Progress (migrating to ddx-agent CONTRACT-003)
11	**Priority:** P0
12	**Owner:** DDx Team
13	
14	## Overview
15	
16	DDx invokes LLMs through the `ddx-agent` module, defined by
17	**CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service** in the `~/Projects/agent` repo
18	(`docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md`).
19	
20	That contract is the entire boundary. DDx's CLI commands (`ddx agent run`,
21	`ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx work`, `ddx agent providers`, `ddx agent models`,
22	`ddx agent route-status`, `ddx agent check`) are thin wrappers that call into
23	the `agentlib.DdxAgent` interface and render its results.
24	
25	## DDx-side responsibilities
26	
27	DDx owns the bead-driven workflow surface. It does NOT own:
28	- Harness orchestration (claude, codex, opencode, pi, gemini, native)
29	- Provider routing
30	- Model catalog
31	- Compaction
32	- Tool registration
33	- Session-log shape
34	
35	Those all live inside ddx-agent per CONTRACT-003.
36	
37	DDx owns:
38	
39	- **Bead-driven invocation.** `ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx work`, and the
40	  server's queue-drain worker translate bead state into `ExecuteRequest`
41	  values and surface results back into the bead tracker.
42	- **Execute-bead orchestration.** Worktree creation, base-revision pinning,
43	  result landing (merge / preserve / no-changes), gate evaluation, evidence
44	  bundle capture. The agent provides the LLM execution; DDx provides the
45	  git-aware orchestration.
46	- **Evidence and session capture.** DDx writes `.ddx/executions/<id>/`
47	  bundles with prompts, manifests, and result artifacts. The agent's session
48	  log path (returned in `ExecuteResponse.SessionLogPath`) is captured into
49	  the bundle.
50	- **Profile policy at the request level.** DDx selects `Profile`, `Effort`,
51	  `Permissions` per bead (or per CLI invocation) based on bead metadata
52	  and user input. The agent receives those as `ExecuteRequest` fields and
53	  performs the routing.
54	
55	## Profile Routing
56	
57	`ddx work --profile default` is the primary queue-drain invocation. DDx maps
58	the user-facing profile to an ordered tier ladder and resolves each tier through
59	the agent service with the configured provider affinity and capability gating.
60	
61	| Profile | Ladder | Intent |
62	| --- | --- | --- |
63	| `default` | `[cheap, standard, smart]` | Local first, then subscription cloud, then high-quality cloud when earlier tiers fail. This is the common throughput-per-dollar path. |
64	| `cheap` | `[cheap]` | Local only. Never escalates; if local cannot serve the bead, the bead is unclaimed and waits. |
65	| `fast` | `[fast, smart]` | Cloud-fast first, skipping local warmup; escalates to smart on failure. |
66	| `smart` | `[smart]` | High-quality cloud from the start. No escalation. |
67	
68	The `.ddx/config.yaml` field `agent.routing.profile_ladders` can override the
69	ordered tier list per profile. The legacy flat `agent.routing.profile_priority`
70	is still read as the `default` profile fallback and emits a deprecation warning;
71	new configs should use `profile_ladders.default`. `agent.routing.model_overrides`
72	can map a ladder tier such as `cheap`, `standard`, `fast`, or `smart` to a
73	concrete model reference before DDx asks the agent service to resolve the route.
74	
75	Escalation advances to the next tier for `execution_failed`,
76	`land_conflict`, `post_run_check_failed`, and
77	`structural_validation_failed`. `no_changes` keeps the existing cooldown and
78	satisfaction-adjudication path rather than consuming a higher tier.
79	
80	## Asking ddx-agent for changes
81	
82	When DDx needs new behavior from the agent — a new method, a new field on
83	`ExecuteRequest`, a new event type, a new policy knob — file a PR against
84	CONTRACT-003 in the agent repo. Maintainers decide whether the surface grows.
85	
86	Do not import agent internal packages. They live under `internal/` and the
87	Go compiler blocks external imports after agent v0.5.0 ships.
88	
89	## Migration status
90	
91	DDx is currently migrating from the legacy DDx-internal routing/harness
92	implementation to the CONTRACT-003 surface. Migration epic: `ddx-ac5c7fdb`.
93	Pre-migration, FEAT-006 documented harness orchestration responsibilities
94	that have since moved upstream.
95	
96	## Design References
97	
98	- `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-006-agent-session-capture.md` — session log shape and evidence bundles
99	- `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-013-multi-agent-coordination.md` — worktree-aware dispatch, concurrent bead safety
100	- `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-014-token-awareness.md` — provider availability, quota, and signal model
101	- `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-016-process-metrics.md` — bead-level cost and rework derivation
102	- `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md` — host+user coordinator scoping
103	- `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-021-service-backed-multi-node-topology.md` — service-backed node topology

Excellent! Now let me compile my findings into a comprehensive report:

## Summary: Execute-Loop Implementation in DDx Go Codebase

I've thoroughly explored the DDx Go codebase and found the execute-loop/execute-bead implementation. Here are the key files and findings:

### PRIMARY FILES

#### 1. **CLI Command Layer** - `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-0097af14-20260429T182135-7183abb2/cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go` (Lines 1-262)

This file defines the `execute-bead` CLI command with:
- Usage: `ddx agent execute-bead <bead-id>`
- Flags: `--project`, `--from`, `--no-merge`, `--harness`, `--model`, `--provider`, `--model-ref`, `--effort`, `--context-budget`, `--prompt`, `--json`
- Two-phase flow: Worker step (runs agent in isolated worktree) + Orchestrator step (lands result via merge/preserve/ff)
- Result status codes: `success`, `no_changes`, `already_satisfied`, `land_conflict`, `post_run_check_failed`, `execution_failed`, `structural_validation_failed`

#### 2. **Landing Coordinator** - `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-0097af14-20260429T182135-7183abb2/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` (Lines 1-914)

Core landing logic for merging worker results into target branch:
- **Fast-path (FF)**: When target tip == BaseRev, direct fast-forward via `update-ref` (worker's commit becomes new tip unchanged)
- **Merge-path**: When target advanced, creates merge commit combining worker result with new target tip
- **Preserve-ref**: On merge conflict, saves ResultRev under `refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>`
- **Push handling**: Initial push, then one auto-recovery attempt (fetch + merge + retry) on conflict
- **Key structs**: `LandRequest`, `LandResult`, `PreClaimResult`, `LandingGitOps` interface

**Key function - `landPushAutoRecover` (Lines 791-832)**:
- Attempts fetch + merge + retry-push when initial push fails
- Returns `recovered=true` on successful retry-push
- Returns `landPushAutoMergeConflictError` when auto-merge hits unresolvable conflict
- Used by `landPush` (Lines 732-757) which sets `PushRecovered=true` on success or `PushConflict=true` on merge conflict

**Evidence tracking**:
- `landEvidence` (Lines 563-578) creates trailing execution-evidence commit after successful land
- Evidence commit is a normal child of result's NewTip, agent's original commit SHA never amended

#### 3. **Execute-Bead Loop Worker** - `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-0097af14-20260429T182135-7183abb2/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` (Lines 1-1198)

Main queue-draining worker with structured cooldown, review, and escalation handling:

**Key constants**:
- `MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour` (Line 57) - Upper bound on auto-set cooldowns
- `DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3` (Line 49) - Retry limit per result_rev before `review-manual-required`

**Key function - `capLoopCooldown` (Lines 59-70)**:
```go
func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
    if d <= 0 { return 0 }
    if d > MaxLoopCooldown { return MaxLoopCooldown }
    return d
}
```
Used in every `SetExecutionCooldown` call to prevent auto-parking beads for >24h.

**Key function - `adjudicateNoChanges` (Lines 1059-1086)**:
- Closed when rationale is "specific" (cites commit SHA or test name) OR count >= maxNoChangesBeforeClose
- Verifies AC (acceptance criteria) claims before closing to prevent false closes
- Returns `(satisfied bool, evidence string, error)`

**Loop flow (Lines 269-872 - `Run` method)**:
1. Claims ready bead from store
2. Pre-claim hook (fetch origin + ancestry check)
3. Routing preflight gate (validates harness/model compatibility)
4. Spawns heartbeat goroutine
5. Calls Executor (agent runner)
6. Routes on result status:
   - **`success`**: Runs optional post-merge review, closes on APPROVE, reopens on REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK
   - **`no_changes`**: Adjudicates satisfaction or applies cooldown
   - **`declined_needs_decomposition`**: Records event + parks for 24h cap
   - **`push_failed`/`push_conflict`**: Parks for 24h cap (distinct signals)
   - **other failures**: Unclairms + applies optional cooldown
7. Appends event to bead
8. Emits terminal progress phase

**Review handling (Lines 505-657)**:
- Per-result_rev retry counter (counts prior `review-error` events with same result_rev)
- On Nth failure (default N=3): Emits `review-manual-required` event, parks bead for 24h
- Verdict routing: APPROVE → close, REQUEST_CHANGES → reopen, BLOCK → reopen with flag

**Cooldown capping examples**:
- Line 548: `parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))` for review-manual-required
- Line 758: `parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))` for push_failed
- Line 703: `retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(noProgressCooldown))` for no-progress

### RELATED CONCEPTS FOUND

**`land_conflict`**: Preserved status returned by landing logic when merge fails (Line 684 in execute_bead_land.go: `"merge conflict"`)

**`execution_failed`**: Status code for agent/harness errors (Lines 66, 402, 476, 483 in agent_execute_bead.go and execute_bead_loop.go)

**`preserved iteration`** & **`refs/ddx/iterations`**: Hidden refs storing conflicted results at path format `refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>` (Line 552 in execute_bead_land.go, `landIterationRef` function)

**No explicit `landPushAutoRecover` definitions** in execute_bead_loop.go, but it's used from execute_bead_land.go and called via `landPush` flow.

### CHANGELOG EXCERPT (Lines 1-100)

Key recent changes (Unreleased section):
- **Push-race auto-recovery (Bead ddx-a458af7c)**: New `landPushAutoRecover` with fetch+merge+retry when push rejected
- **`push_conflict` distinct outcome**: New status separate from `push_failed`; triggers structured `kind:push-conflict` bead event
- **Loop-set cooldown cap at 24h**: `capLoopCooldown` clamps all auto-set cooldowns; operators can still extend manually beyond 24h
- Breaking change: `agent.routing.default_harness` removed; use top-level `agent.harness` instead
- Opt-in fields: `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` now require `--escalate` and `--override-model` flags respectively

### FEAT-006 SPEC (Lines 1-110)

**Feature: DDx Agent Service** - Defines DDx's consumer responsibilities around LLM orchestration:
- DDx owns: bead-driven invocation, execute-bead orchestration (worktree, merging, gates), evidence capture, profile policy
- Agent owns: harness orchestration, provider routing, model catalog, compaction, tool registration, session logs
- Profile ladder routing: `default=[cheap,standard,smart]`, `cheap=[cheap]`, `fast=[fast,smart]`, `smart=[smart]`
- Escalation advances on: `execution_failed`, `land_conflict`, `post_run_check_failed`, `structural_validation_failed`
- Status: Migrating from internal routing to CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service interface

### FILE PATHS (Complete Reference)

**CLI Commands**:
- `/cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go` (262 lines) - execute-bead command
- `/cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop.go` - Missing from glob results; likely contains `execute-loop` command wrapper

**Agent Implementation**:
- `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` (914 lines) - Landing coordinator
- `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` (1198 lines) - Loop worker
- `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go` - Worker orchestration
- `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go` - Status classification
- `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go` - Post-merge review logic

**Documentation**:
- `/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md` (110 lines) - Feature spec
- `/CHANGELOG.md` - Full changelog with push-race and cooldown features

**Tests** (30+ test files covering execute-loop, landing, review, push-failure, routing, etc.)

1	package agent
2	
3	// execute_bead_land.go — the land coordinator pattern.
4	//
5	// This file implements the human-PR landing model for execute-bead results.
6	// The old Merge() path in execute_bead_orchestrator.go has been deleted; all
7	// target-ref writes now flow through Land(), which is called exactly once per
8	// submission by a per-project coordinator goroutine (see
9	// internal/server/workers.go:LandCoordinator).
10	//
11	// The flow mirrors how a human merges PRs:
12	//
13	//   1. Fetch the current target tip (from origin when a remote exists, or
14	//      from the local branch otherwise).
15	//   2. If the current tip equals the worker's BaseRev — fast-forward the
16	//      target branch directly to the worker's ResultRev via update-ref. The
17	//      worker's commit keeps its original parent; no new commit is created.
18	//   3. Otherwise — the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
19	//      a temporary detached worktree at the current target tip, run
20	//      `git merge --no-ff` to introduce the worker's ResultRev, and
21	//      fast-forward the target branch to the resulting merge commit. The
22	//      worker's commit is NEVER rewritten: its parent is still BaseRev, so
23	//      a later replay observes the same inputs the worker saw.
24	//   4. If the merge conflicts — abort cleanly, preserve the original
25	//      ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>,
26	//      and return preserved status. Target branch is never modified.
27	//   5. If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip. The push is
28	//      strictly fast-forward; push failures are reported via PushFailed but
29	//      do not roll back the local target ref.
30	//
31	// Replay fidelity is the reason for merge-over-rebase: a rebased commit has
32	// a rewritten parent that lies about what the worker saw at execution time.
33	// A merge commit preserves both histories — the worker's original commit
34	// (parent = BaseRev) and the target's prior tip — losslessly.
35	//
36	// The coordinator owning the goroutine provides the serialization guarantee,
37	// so Land() itself does not take any locks.
38	
39	import (
40		"context"
41		"fmt"
42		"os"
43		"path/filepath"
44		"strings"
45	
46		internalgit "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
47	)
48	
49	// LandRequest is one submission to the land coordinator: "here is the worker's
50	// result from base B to result R for bead X; land it on the project's target
51	// branch."
52	type LandRequest struct {
53		// WorktreeDir is the path to the project's repository directory (the
54		// directory the coordinator operates on). The original worker worktree
55		// has typically already been removed by the time Land() runs — Land()
56		// creates its own temporary worktrees when a merge is needed.
57		WorktreeDir string
58	
59		// BaseRev is the revision the worker branched off when it started.
60		// When the current target tip equals BaseRev, Land() takes the fast path.
61		BaseRev string
62	
63		// ResultRev is the worker's final commit SHA. Must be reachable in the
64		// project's git object store at the time Land() is called.
65		ResultRev string
66	
67		// BeadID identifies the bead this submission is for. Used to build the
68		// preserve-ref path on conflict.
69		BeadID string
70	
71		// AttemptID uniquely identifies this land attempt. Used to build the
72		// preserve-ref path on conflict so concurrent attempts for the same
73		// bead do not collide.
74		AttemptID string
75	
76		// TargetBranch is the branch to advance. When empty, Land() resolves
77		// the project's current HEAD branch and uses that.
78		TargetBranch string
79	
80		// EvidenceDir is the relative path to the per-attempt execution evidence
81		// directory (e.g. ".ddx/executions/20260416T181205-b5419982"). When
82		// non-empty and the main land succeeds, Land() creates a trailing
83		// evidence commit that folds these files into the target branch. The
84		// agent's commit SHA is NOT amended — the evidence commit is a separate
85		// child commit, preserving closing_commit_sha references.
86		EvidenceDir string
87	}
88	
89	// LandResult describes the outcome of a single Land() call.
90	type LandResult struct {
91		// Status is one of:
92		//   - "landed":    the target branch now points at a new commit
93		//                  (either ResultRev itself on the fast-forward path,
94		//                  or a merge commit on the merge path).
95		//   - "preserved": the merge conflicted; ResultRev is saved under
96		//                  PreserveRef and the target branch is unchanged.
97		//   - "no-changes": ResultRev == BaseRev; nothing to land.
98		Status string
99	
100		// NewTip is the new value of the target branch when Status == "landed".
101		// On the fast-forward path NewTip == ResultRev; on the merge path NewTip
102		// is the SHA of the merge commit (whose parents are the prior target
103		// tip and ResultRev). Empty when preserved or no-changes.
104		NewTip string
105	
106		// Merged is true when the land took the merge-commit path (current tip
107		// had advanced beyond BaseRev, so Land() created a merge commit to
108		// combine the worker's result with the new target tip). False on the
109		// fast-forward path where the worker's commit became the new tip
110		// unchanged.
111		Merged bool
112	
113		// PreserveRef is set when Status == "preserved". It names the ref under
114		// refs/ddx/iterations/ where ResultRev was saved for later recovery.
115		PreserveRef string
116	
117		// Reason is a human-readable explanation, especially useful when
118		// Status == "preserved" (e.g. "merge conflict") or when PushFailed.
119		Reason string
120	
121		// PushFailed is true when the local target ref was advanced successfully
122		// but the subsequent push to origin was rejected (e.g. non-fast-forward).
123		// The local state is authoritative; the remote will need to be
124		// reconciled by a later land or an operator.
125		PushFailed bool
126	
127		// PushError captures the underlying push error when PushFailed is true.
128		PushError string
129	
130		// PushConflict is true when the initial push was rejected (e.g. origin
131		// advanced) and the loop's automatic pull/merge/retry recovery ran into
132		// a merge conflict it cannot resolve without operator input. Distinct
133		// from PushFailed so the loop can park the bead for human review under
134		// a short cooldown instead of treating it as a generic push failure.
135		PushConflict bool
136	
137		// PushRecovered is true when the initial push was rejected but the loop
138		// successfully fetched, merged, and re-pushed without operator input.
139		// The land is fully complete; PushFailed and PushConflict are false.
140		// Set so callers can record telemetry on how often the auto-recovery
141		// path actually saves an operator round-trip.
142		PushRecovered bool
143	
144		// MergedCommitCount is the number of commits reachable from ResultRev but
145		// not from BaseRev — i.e. the "size" of the worker's contribution being
146		// merged in. Zero on the no-changes path. Set on both the fast-forward
147		// and merge-commit paths so metrics can compare contribution sizes.
148		MergedCommitCount int
149	
150		// EvidenceCommitSHA is set when a trailing execution-evidence commit was
151		// created after the main land. When set, NewTip points at this commit
152		// (not at the original agent commit or merge commit).
153		EvidenceCommitSHA string
154	}
155	
156	// PreClaimResult is the outcome of a FetchOriginAncestryCheck call.
157	type PreClaimResult struct {
158		// Action is one of:
159		//   "unchanged"     — local tip == origin tip, no action taken
160		//   "fast-forwarded"— local was behind origin; local branch was advanced
161		//   "no-origin"     — no origin remote; check skipped
162		//   "local-ahead"   — local is ahead of origin; no action needed
163		//   "diverged"      — neither is ancestor of the other; claim should be aborted
164		Action    string
165		LocalSHA  string
166		OriginSHA string
167	}
168	
169	// LandingGitOps abstracts the git operations Land() needs. RealLandingGitOps
170	// shells out to git; tests inject fakes or run against real temp repos.
171	type LandingGitOps interface {
172		// HasRemote reports whether the given remote name exists in dir.
173		HasRemote(dir, remote string) bool
174	
175		// CurrentBranch returns the branch HEAD currently points at in dir, or
176		// an error if HEAD is detached or unresolvable.
177		CurrentBranch(dir string) (string, error)
178	
179		// FetchBranch fetches remote/branch into dir (no merge, no checkout).
180		// Returns nil when no remote exists.
181		FetchBranch(dir, remote, branch string) error
182	
183		// ResolveRef resolves ref (e.g. "refs/heads/main" or "origin/main") to a
184		// commit SHA. When ref is unresolvable returns ("", error).
185		ResolveRef(dir, ref string) (string, error)
186	
187		// UpdateRefTo updates ref in dir to sha. When oldSHA is non-empty, the
188		// update is conditional on the current ref value equalling oldSHA.
189		UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA string) error
190	
191		// SyncWorkTreeToHead syncs the index AND the working-tree files in dir
192		// to HEAD after a non-checkout ref update (e.g. update-ref). fromRev is
193		// the commit HEAD pointed at BEFORE the ref update; it is used to
194		// compute the minimal set of tracked files changed by the update so
195		// that unrelated local modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat
196		// writes, operator scratch) are NOT clobbered.
197		//
198		// Needed because Land() advances the target ref via update-ref, which
199		// touches neither the index nor the worktree. Before this fix, Land()
200		// only ran `git read-tree HEAD` to sync the index — leaving the main
201		// worktree showing phantom deleted/modified entries for every file the
202		// landed commit touched, and subsequent agents reading files from disk
203		// would see stale content.
204		//
205		// Implementation: `git read-tree HEAD` + `git diff --name-only fromRev
206		// HEAD` to enumerate changed files + `git checkout-index -f --` to
207		// materialize them from the freshly-synced index.
208		SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error
209	
210		// AddWorktree creates a new worktree at path checked out at rev in dir.
211		AddWorktree(dir, path, rev string) error
212	
213		// RemoveWorktree removes the worktree at path in dir (force).
214		RemoveWorktree(dir, path string) error
215	
216		// MergeInto runs `git merge --no-ff -m msg srcRev` inside wtDir, which
217		// must already be checked out at the current target tip. A clean merge
218		// produces a merge commit whose parents are [currentTip, srcRev]; the
219		// commit SHA can be read with HeadRevAt. Returns nil on clean merge,
220		// or an error on conflict. On error, the implementation is responsible
221		// for running `git merge --abort` so the worktree is left clean.
222		MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error
223	
224		// HeadRevAt returns HEAD of the git workdir at dir.
225		HeadRevAt(dir string) (string, error)
226	
227		// PushFFOnly pushes localRef to remote as targetBranch with strict
228		// fast-forward semantics. Returns an error when the push would not be
229		// fast-forward or when the network call fails.
