232		// FetchOriginAncestryCheck fetches origin/targetBranch and compares it
233		// to the local branch tip. When origin is ahead the local branch is
234		// fast-forwarded via update-ref + read-tree. When the two tips have
235		// diverged (neither is ancestor of the other) the returned result has
236		// Action == "diverged". When no origin remote exists the result has
237		// Action == "no-origin". The caller decides whether to abort a claim.
238		FetchOriginAncestryCheck(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error)
239	
240		// CountCommits returns the number of commits reachable from tip but not
241		// from base (i.e. git rev-list --count base..tip). Used to record the
242		// contribution size in land metrics. Returns 0 on error.
243		CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int
244	
245		// StageDir stages all files under relPath in dir for the next commit.
246		StageDir(dir, relPath string) error
247	
248		// CommitStaged creates a commit from currently staged changes using msg
249		// as the commit message. Returns (sha, nil) when a commit was made,
250		// ("", nil) when nothing was staged, and ("", err) on failure.
251		CommitStaged(dir, msg string) (string, error)
252	}
253	
254	// RealLandingGitOps implements LandingGitOps via os/exec git commands.
255	type RealLandingGitOps struct{}
256	
257	func (RealLandingGitOps) HasRemote(dir, remote string) bool {
258		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "remote").Output()
259		if err != nil {
260			return false
261		}
262		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), "\n") {
263			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == remote {
264				return true
265			}
266		}
267		return false
268	}
269	
270	func (RealLandingGitOps) CurrentBranch(dir string) (string, error) {
271		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "symbolic-ref", "--short", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
272		if err != nil {
273			return "", fmt.Errorf("git symbolic-ref HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
274		}
275		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
276	}
277	
278	func (RealLandingGitOps) FetchBranch(dir, remote, branch string) error {
279		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "fetch", remote, branch).CombinedOutput()
280		if err != nil {
281			return fmt.Errorf("git fetch %s %s: %s: %w", remote, branch, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
282		}
283		return nil
284	}
285	
286	func (RealLandingGitOps) ResolveRef(dir, ref string) (string, error) {
287		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", ref).CombinedOutput()
288		if err != nil {
289			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
290		}
291		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
292	}
293	
294	func (RealLandingGitOps) UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA string) error {
295		args := []string{"update-ref", ref, sha}
296		if oldSHA != "" {
297			args = append(args, oldSHA)
298		}
299		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).CombinedOutput()
300		if err != nil {
301			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
302		}
303		return nil
304	}
305	
306	func (RealLandingGitOps) SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error {
307		// Step 1: sync the index to HEAD. This is required before checkout-index
308		// below will do anything useful, and also keeps subsequent CommitTracker
309		// calls from building stale trees.
310		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "read-tree", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
311		if err != nil {
312			return fmt.Errorf("git read-tree HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
313		}
314	
315		// Step 2: compute the list of tracked files changed between the previous
316		// HEAD and the current HEAD. These are the files that the landing commit
317		// added, modified, or deleted. We only restore THESE files to avoid
318		// clobbering unrelated local modifications (agent-logs being written by
319		// the running server, beads.jsonl heartbeat updates, operator scratch).
320		if fromRev == "" {
321			// No previous HEAD known — fall back to the unsafe behaviour of
322			// read-tree only. Acceptable when the caller is a best-effort path
323			// that cannot reconstruct fromRev.
324			return nil
325		}
326		diffOut, diffErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--name-only", fromRev, "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
327		if diffErr != nil {
328			// Diff failed (bad fromRev, shallow history, etc.) — leave the
329			// worktree stale rather than risk a broken checkout. The CommitTracker
330			// stale-tree bug is the prior status quo and no worse than before.
331			return nil
332		}
333		changed := strings.Fields(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut)))
334		if len(changed) == 0 {
335			return nil
336		}
337	
338		// Step 3: split into existing-in-HEAD (checkout-index) and
339		// deleted-in-HEAD (os.Remove) buckets. checkout-index only writes files
340		// that are in the index; it cannot represent a deletion, so we handle
341		// those ourselves.
342		var indexFiles []string
343		var removedFiles []string
344		for _, f := range changed {
345			probe := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "ls-files", "--error-unmatch", "--", f)
346			if probe.Run() == nil {
347				indexFiles = append(indexFiles, f)
348			} else {
349				removedFiles = append(removedFiles, f)
350			}
351		}
352	
353		// Step 4: materialize the index-present files into the working tree.
354		// -f overwrites any stale content at these exact paths. Unrelated files
355		// are untouched because we pass the specific path list.
356		if len(indexFiles) > 0 {
357			args := []string{"checkout-index", "-f", "--"}
358			args = append(args, indexFiles...)
359			out2, err2 := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).CombinedOutput()
360			if err2 != nil {
361				return fmt.Errorf("git checkout-index -f: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out2)), err2)
362			}
363		}
364	
365		// Step 5: remove files that the landing commit deleted and whose removal
366		// did not propagate to the worktree because update-ref bypassed the
367		// working-tree update.
368		for _, f := range removedFiles {
369			full := filepath.Join(dir, f)
370			_ = os.Remove(full) // best-effort; leave the file if removal fails
371		}
372	
373		return nil
374	}
375	
376	func (RealLandingGitOps) AddWorktree(dir, path, rev string) error {
377		// --detach so the worktree does not create a persistent branch.
378		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "add", "--force", "--detach", path, rev).CombinedOutput()
379		if err != nil {
380			return fmt.Errorf("git worktree add %s %s: %s: %w", path, rev, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
381		}
382		return nil
383	}
384	
385	func (RealLandingGitOps) RemoveWorktree(dir, path string) error {
386		_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "remove", "--force", path).Run()
387		_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "prune").Run()
388		return nil
389	}
390	
391	func (RealLandingGitOps) MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error {
392		// --no-ff forces a merge commit even when the merge could fast-forward
393		// (which shouldn't happen given our caller's ancestry check, but is a
394		// defensive guarantee that target history always gets a marker commit).
395		// We inject user.name/user.email via -c so the merge commit can be
396		// created even when the worktree inherited no git config; the
397		// coordinator is a machine actor and should not adopt a human's identity.
398		out, err := internalgit.Command(
399			context.Background(), wtDir,
400			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
401			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
402			"merge", "--no-ff", "-m", msg, srcRev,
403		).CombinedOutput()
404		if err != nil {
405			_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wtDir, "merge", "--abort").Run()
406			return fmt.Errorf("git merge --no-ff %s: %s: %w", srcRev, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
407		}
408		return nil
409	}
410	
411	func (RealLandingGitOps) HeadRevAt(dir string) (string, error) {
412		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
413		if err != nil {
414			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
415		}
416		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
417	}
418	
419	func (RealLandingGitOps) PushFFOnly(dir, remote, localRef, targetBranch string) error {
420		// Refspec "<local>:<remote>" with no '+' prefix → fast-forward only.
421		refspec := fmt.Sprintf("%s:refs/heads/%s", localRef, targetBranch)
422		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "push", remote, refspec).CombinedOutput()
423		if err != nil {
424			return fmt.Errorf("git push %s %s: %s: %w", remote, refspec, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
425		}
426		return nil
427	}
428	
429	func (RealLandingGitOps) CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int {
430		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-list", "--count", base+".."+tip).CombinedOutput()
431		if err != nil {
432			return 0
433		}
434		s := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
435		n := 0
436		for _, c := range s {
437			if c >= '0' && c <= '9' {
438				n = n*10 + int(c-'0')
439			}
440		}
441		return n
442	}
443	
444	func (RealLandingGitOps) StageDir(dir, relPath string) error {
445		// Exclude embedded session logs from the evidence commit — tracking
446		// multi-thousand-line .jsonl files caused retry review prompts to
447		// balloon past 2M tokens and crash every provider with n_keep > n_ctx
448		// (ddx-39e27896). manifest.json, result.json, prompt.md, and
449		// usage.json remain tracked for audit; the raw session log lives on
450		// disk, not in git history.
451		args := append([]string{"add", "--", relPath}, EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs()...)
452		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).CombinedOutput()
453		if err != nil {
454			return fmt.Errorf("git add %s: %s: %w", relPath, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
455		}
456		return nil
457	}
458	
459	func (RealLandingGitOps) CommitStaged(dir, msg string) (string, error) {
460		out, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--name-only").Output()
461		if len(strings.TrimSpace(string(out))) == 0 {
462			return "", nil
463		}
464		commitOut, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir,
465			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
466			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
467			"commit", "-m", msg,
468		).CombinedOutput()
469		if err != nil {
470			return "", fmt.Errorf("git commit: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut)), err)
471		}
472		shaOut, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "HEAD").Output()
473		if err != nil {
474			return "", fmt.Errorf("rev-parse HEAD after evidence commit: %w", err)
475		}
476		return strings.TrimSpace(string(shaOut)), nil
477	}
478	
479	// FetchOriginAncestryCheck implements LandingGitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck.
480	func (RealLandingGitOps) FetchOriginAncestryCheck(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error) {
481		// Step 1: check for origin remote.
482		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "remote", "get-url", "origin").CombinedOutput()
483		if err != nil {
484			// No origin remote — single-machine case; skip check.
485			return PreClaimResult{Action: "no-origin"}, nil
486		}
487		_ = out // remote URL not needed
488	
489		// Step 2: fetch origin/targetBranch (best-effort; failure is non-fatal but surfaced).
490		if fetchOut, fetchErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "fetch", "origin", targetBranch).CombinedOutput(); fetchErr != nil {
491			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("git fetch origin %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(fetchOut)), fetchErr)
492		}
493	
494		// Step 3: resolve local tip.
495		localOut, localErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/heads/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
496		if localErr != nil {
497			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving local %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut)), localErr)
498		}
499		localSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut))
500	
501		// Step 4: resolve origin tip.
502		originOut, originErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/remotes/origin/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
503		if originErr != nil {
504			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving origin/%s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut)), originErr)
505		}
506		originSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut))
507	
508		// Step 5: compare.
509		if localSHA == originSHA {
510			return PreClaimResult{Action: "unchanged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
511		}
512	
513		// Is local an ancestor of origin? (origin is ahead)
514		localAncestorErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", localSHA, originSHA).Run()
515		if localAncestorErr == nil {
516			// Origin is ahead: fast-forward local branch via update-ref + sync worktree.
517			targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
518			if upErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", targetRef, originSHA, localSHA).Run(); upErr != nil {
519				return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, originSHA, upErr)
520			}
521			// Sync index + working tree to new HEAD so the main worktree files
522			// reflect the pulled-down origin commits. Pass localSHA as fromRev
523			// to restrict the restore to files actually changed by origin's
524			// advance, preserving unrelated local modifications.
525			_ = (RealLandingGitOps{}).SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, localSHA)
526			return PreClaimResult{Action: "fast-forwarded", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
527		}
528	
529		// Is origin an ancestor of local? (local is ahead)
530		originAncestorErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", originSHA, localSHA).Run()
531		if originAncestorErr == nil {
532			return PreClaimResult{Action: "local-ahead", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
533		}
534	
535		// Neither is ancestor of the other: diverged.
536		return PreClaimResult{Action: "diverged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
537	}
538	
539	// landIterationRef returns the documented hidden ref for a land-time preserve.
540	// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>. The short-tip
541	// captures the current target tip at the time of the conflict so subsequent
542	// recovery tools can reconstruct which merge target was in play.
543	func landIterationRef(beadID, attemptID, tip string) string {
544		short := tip
545		if len(short) > 12 {
546			short = short[:12]
547		}
548		attempt := attemptID
549		if attempt == "" {
550			attempt = NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
551		}
552		return fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/iterations/%s/%s-%s", beadID, attempt, short)
553	}
554	
555	// landEvidence creates a trailing commit that folds the per-attempt execution
556	// evidence directory into the target branch. Called after the main land (ff or
557	// merge) succeeds and before the push. The evidence commit is a normal child of
558	// result.NewTip — the agent's original commit SHA is not amended.
559	//
560	// Best-effort: only works when HEAD is a symbolic ref to targetBranch (the
561	// normal case for server workers and CLI users). When HEAD is detached or
562	// points elsewhere, the evidence commit is silently skipped.
563	func landEvidence(wd, targetBranch string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps, result *LandResult) {
564		branch, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
565		if err != nil || branch != targetBranch {
566			return
567		}
568		if err := gitOps.StageDir(wd, req.EvidenceDir); err != nil {
569			return
570		}
571		msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: add execution evidence [%s]", shortAttempt(req.AttemptID))
572		sha, err := gitOps.CommitStaged(wd, msg)
573		if err != nil || sha == "" {
574			return
575		}
576		result.EvidenceCommitSHA = sha
577		result.NewTip = sha
578	}
579	
580	// Land performs fetch → (ff or merge) → push for a single submission.
581	// Callers MUST serialize calls per projectRoot (the server coordinator
582	// goroutine provides this). Land() itself takes no internal locks.
583	//
584	// projectRoot is the directory containing the project's .git. req.WorktreeDir,
585	// when non-empty, is used as the git working directory for all commands; when
586	// empty, projectRoot is used. Both usually refer to the same dir — the field
587	// is kept for forward compatibility with multi-clone topologies.
588	func Land(projectRoot string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps) (*LandResult, error) {
589		if gitOps == nil {
590			gitOps = RealLandingGitOps{}
591		}
592		wd := req.WorktreeDir
593		if wd == "" {
594			wd = projectRoot
595		}
596	
597		// Trivial guard — no commits to land.
598		if req.ResultRev == "" || req.ResultRev == req.BaseRev {
599			return &LandResult{Status: "no-changes", Reason: "result_rev == base_rev"}, nil
600		}
601	
602		// Resolve target branch (default to project HEAD).
603		targetBranch := req.TargetBranch
604		if targetBranch == "" {
605			br, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
606			if err != nil {
607				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target branch: %w", err)
608			}
609			targetBranch = br
610		}
611		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
612	
613		// Fetch the current target tip from origin when available; otherwise
614		// read the local branch directly.
615		hasOrigin := gitOps.HasRemote(wd, "origin")
616		if hasOrigin {
617			// Fetch is best-effort: a disconnected remote must not block
618			// a local land. Log-style errors are swallowed here; the
619			// subsequent ResolveRef will surface any fatal issue.
620			_ = gitOps.FetchBranch(wd, "origin", targetBranch)
621		}
622		currentTip, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, targetRef)
623		if err != nil {
624			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target tip %s: %w", targetRef, err)
625		}
626	
627		contribCount := gitOps.CountCommits(wd, req.BaseRev, req.ResultRev)
628	
629		// Fast path: no sibling advanced the target branch → straight ff via
630		// update-ref. The worker's commit becomes the new tip unchanged; its
631		// parent is still BaseRev, so replay sees the same inputs the worker
632		// saw. No merge commit is created.
633		if currentTip == req.BaseRev {
634			if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, req.ResultRev, currentTip); err != nil {
635				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, req.ResultRev, err)
636			}
637			// Refresh the main worktree's index + working-tree files so the
638			// operator and subsequent agents see the newly-landed commit's
639			// changes. Pass currentTip as fromRev so only files actually
640			// changed by this commit are restored — unrelated local
641			// modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat) are preserved.
642			// Non-fatal on error.
643			_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(wd, currentTip)
644			result := &LandResult{
645				Status:            "landed",
646				NewTip:            req.ResultRev,
647				Merged:            false,
648				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
649			}
650			if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
651				landEvidence(wd, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result)
652			}
653			landPush(wd, targetBranch, result.NewTip, hasOrigin, gitOps, result)
654			return result, nil
655		}
656	
657		// Merge path: the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
658		// a temp detached worktree at currentTip and run `git merge --no-ff
659		// ResultRev` inside it. The result is a merge commit whose parents are
660		// [currentTip, ResultRev]. Crucially, ResultRev itself is NOT rewritten:
661		// its parent is still BaseRev, so replay observes the original inputs.
662		tempWT, tempWtErr := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-land-wt-*")
663		if tempWtErr != nil {
664			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating temp worktree dir: %w", tempWtErr)
665		}
666		// os.MkdirTemp creates the dir, but git worktree add refuses to run if
667		// the target already exists. Remove it first so git can recreate it.
668		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
669		if err := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, currentTip); err != nil {
670			return nil, fmt.Errorf("adding temp worktree at %s: %w", currentTip, err)
671		}
672		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
673	
674		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge bead %s attempt %s into %s", req.BeadID, shortAttempt(req.AttemptID), targetBranch)
675		if err := gitOps.MergeInto(tempWT, req.ResultRev, mergeMsg); err != nil {
676			// Merge conflict: preserve the original ResultRev and return.
677			preserveRef := landIterationRef(req.BeadID, req.AttemptID, currentTip)
678			if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, preserveRef, req.ResultRev, ""); upErr != nil {
679				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving %s after merge conflict: %w", preserveRef, upErr)
680			}
681			return &LandResult{
682				Status:            "preserved",
683				PreserveRef:       preserveRef,
684				Reason:            "merge conflict",
685				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
686			}, nil
687		}
688	
689		// Merge clean: read the merge commit SHA from the temp worktree's HEAD
690		// and fast-forward the target branch to it.
691		mergeSHA, err := gitOps.HeadRevAt(tempWT)
692		if err != nil {
693			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading merge HEAD: %w", err)
694		}
695		if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, mergeSHA, currentTip); err != nil {
696			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to merge commit %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, err)
697		}
698		// Refresh the main worktree's index + working-tree files. Pass
699		// currentTip as fromRev; the merge commit's tree contains both the
700		// current-tip files and the incoming ResultRev files, so the diff
701		// against currentTip yields exactly the files affected by the merge.
702		// Unrelated local modifications are preserved.
703		_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(wd, currentTip)
704	
705		result := &LandResult{
706			Status:            "landed",
707			NewTip:            mergeSHA,
708			Merged:            true,
709			MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
710		}
711		if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
712			landEvidence(wd, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result)
713		}
714		landPush(wd, targetBranch, result.NewTip, hasOrigin, gitOps, result)
715		return result, nil
716	}
717	
718	// landPush pushes the new target tip to origin when a remote exists. Push
719	// failure is non-fatal for the local land outcome; it is surfaced via
720	// PushFailed/PushError on the result.
721	//
722	// On the first push rejection landPush attempts one automatic recovery —
723	// fetch origin, merge our local tip into the new origin tip, advance the
724	// local target ref, and retry the push. The race-with-remote case (origin
725	// advanced with non-overlapping work between our local merge and our push)
726	// is the common failure in a multi-worker / multi-operator setup and can be
727	// fixed without operator intervention. If the auto-merge produces a conflict
728	// the loop cannot resolve, landPush marks the result PushConflict (a distinct
729	// signal from generic PushFailed) so the caller can park the bead for human
730	// review under a short cooldown rather than the long park used for
731	// non-recoverable push failures.
732	func landPush(wd, targetBranch, newTip string, hasOrigin bool, gitOps LandingGitOps, result *LandResult) {
733		if !hasOrigin {
734			return
735		}
736		firstErr := gitOps.PushFFOnly(wd, "origin", newTip, targetBranch)
737		if firstErr == nil {
738			return
739		}
740		// First push rejected. Try one automatic pull/merge/retry-push pass.
741		if recovered, mergeSHA, recErr := landPushAutoRecover(wd, targetBranch, newTip, gitOps); recovered {
742			result.NewTip = mergeSHA
743			result.PushRecovered = true
744			return
745		} else if recErr != nil && isPushAutoMergeConflict(recErr) {
746			// Auto-merge could not be resolved without operator input.
747			result.PushConflict = true
748			result.PushError = recErr.Error()
749			return
750		}
751		// Either recovery itself failed for non-conflict reasons (network, auth,
752		// fetch error, retry push still rejected) or origin was already at our
753		// tip. Surface the original push error so operator messages match the
754		// historical contract.
755		result.PushFailed = true
756		result.PushError = firstErr.Error()
757	}
758	
759	// landPushAutoMergeConflictError tags a recovery error that originates from
760	// an unresolvable git merge conflict, so landPush can branch on it without
761	// pattern-matching free-form error strings.
762	type landPushAutoMergeConflictError struct{ inner error }
763	
764	func (e *landPushAutoMergeConflictError) Error() string { return e.inner.Error() }
765	func (e *landPushAutoMergeConflictError) Unwrap() error { return e.inner }
766	
767	func isPushAutoMergeConflict(err error) bool {
768		for err != nil {
769			if _, ok := err.(*landPushAutoMergeConflictError); ok {
770				return true
771			}
772			type unwrapper interface{ Unwrap() error }
773			u, ok := err.(unwrapper)
774			if !ok {
775				return false
776			}
777			err = u.Unwrap()
778		}
779		return false
780	}
781	
782	// landPushAutoRecover attempts one fetch + merge + retry-push pass after the
783	// initial push was rejected. Returns recovered=true and the new merge SHA
784	// when the retry-push succeeds; otherwise returns recovered=false plus an
785	// error that indicates whether the recovery hit a merge conflict (wrapped in
786	// landPushAutoMergeConflictError) or some other failure.
787	//
788	// Caller responsibilities (already met by landPush):
789	//   - The local target ref is at newTip and not yet pushed.
790	//   - hasOrigin is true.
791	func landPushAutoRecover(wd, targetBranch, newTip string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (recovered bool, mergeSHA string, err error) {
792		if fetchErr := gitOps.FetchBranch(wd, "origin", targetBranch); fetchErr != nil {
793			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery fetch origin %s: %w", targetBranch, fetchErr)
794		}
795		originSHA, resolveErr := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, "refs/remotes/origin/"+targetBranch)
796		if resolveErr != nil {
797			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery resolve origin/%s: %w", targetBranch, resolveErr)
798		}
799		if originSHA == newTip {
800			// Origin is already at our tip — push must have failed for a reason
801			// other than a non-fast-forward (auth, branch protection, large
802			// blob, etc.). Recovery cannot help.
803			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("origin already at local tip; push failure not recoverable by auto-merge")
804		}
805		tempWT, mkErr := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-push-recover-*")
806		if mkErr != nil {
807			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery temp worktree: %w", mkErr)
808		}
809		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
810		if addErr := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, originSHA); addErr != nil {
811			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery add worktree at %s: %w", originSHA, addErr)
812		}
813		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
814	
815		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge origin/%s into %s after push race", targetBranch, targetBranch)
816		if mergeErr := gitOps.MergeInto(tempWT, newTip, mergeMsg); mergeErr != nil {
817			return false, "", &landPushAutoMergeConflictError{inner: mergeErr}
818		}
819		mergeSHA, headErr := gitOps.HeadRevAt(tempWT)
820		if headErr != nil {
821			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery read merge HEAD: %w", headErr)
822		}
823		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
824		if updErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, mergeSHA, newTip); updErr != nil {
825			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery advance %s to %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, updErr)
826		}
827		_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(wd, newTip)
828		if pushErr := gitOps.PushFFOnly(wd, "origin", mergeSHA, targetBranch); pushErr != nil {
829			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery retry push: %w", pushErr)
830		}
831		return true, mergeSHA, nil
832	}
833	
834	// shortAttempt returns a 10-char slug derived from attemptID for use in temp
835	// branch names. When attemptID is empty, it returns the current timestamp.
836	func shortAttempt(attemptID string) string {
837		if attemptID != "" {
838			if len(attemptID) > 16 {
839				return attemptID[:16]
840			}
841			return attemptID
842		}
843		return NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405")
844	}
845	
846	// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult maps a LandResult onto an
847	// ExecuteBeadResult so the execute-bead loop sees the correct final status.
848	// It is the coordinator-pattern replacement for ApplyLandingToResult.
849	func ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res *ExecuteBeadResult, land *LandResult) {
850		if land == nil || res == nil {
851			return
852		}
853		switch land.Status {
854		case "landed":
855			// Fast-forward or merge commit — either way the target branch now
856			// contains the worker's result. ResultRev's own parent is still
857			// BaseRev so replay fidelity is preserved.
858			res.Outcome = "merged"
859			res.Reason = ""
860			res.PreserveRef = ""
861			if land.Merged {
862				res.Reason = "merged onto current tip"
863			}
864			if land.PushConflict {
865				res.Reason = PushConflictReasonPrefix + " " + land.PushError
866			} else if land.PushFailed {
867				res.Reason = PushFailedReasonPrefix + " " + land.PushError
868			}
869			// NewTip reflects the ref actually on the target branch (either
870			// ResultRev on the ff path or the merge commit SHA on the merge path).
871			if land.NewTip != "" {
872				res.ResultRev = land.NewTip
873			}
874		case "preserved":
875			res.Outcome = "preserved"
876			res.Reason = land.Reason
877			res.PreserveRef = land.PreserveRef
878		case "no-changes":
879			// Only overwrite when the worker itself did not already report
880			// a richer no-changes rationale.
881			if res.Outcome == "" || res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded {
882				res.Outcome = "no-changes"
883			}
884			if res.Reason == "" {
885				res.Reason = land.Reason
886			}
887		}
888		// Re-classify loop-visible status from the landing outcome.
889		reasonForStatus := res.Reason
890		if land.Status == "preserved" {
891			// Route preserve reasons through the land-conflict classifier so the
892			// loop sees land_conflict (not generic success).
893			reasonForStatus = "merge conflict"
894		}
895		res.Status = ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, reasonForStatus)
896		res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, res.Reason, res.Error)
897	}
898	
899	// BuildLandRequestFromResult constructs a LandRequest for the coordinator from
900	// an ExecuteBeadResult. The coordinator always passes projectRoot as the
901	// workdir — the worker's original worktree has already been cleaned up by the
902	// time Land() runs.
903	func BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult) LandRequest {
904		return LandRequest{
905			WorktreeDir:  projectRoot,
906			BaseRev:      res.BaseRev,
907			ResultRev:    res.ResultRev,
908			BeadID:       res.BeadID,
909			AttemptID:    res.AttemptID,
910			TargetBranch: "",
911			EvidenceDir:  res.ExecutionDir,
912		}
913	}

18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool
38		PollInterval   time.Duration
39		NoReview       bool
40		LabelFilter    string
41		SessionID      string
42		WorkerID       string
43		ProjectRoot    string
44	}
45	
46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
50	
51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
60	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
61	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
62	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
63		if d <= 0 {
64			return 0
65		}
66		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
67			return MaxLoopCooldown
68		}
69		return d
70	}

134	// already satisfied and should be closed, or is still unresolved and should

268	
269	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
270		if w.Store == nil {
271			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
272		}
273		if w.Executor == nil {
274			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
275		}
276	
277		now := w.Now
278		if now == nil {
279			now = time.Now
280		}
281		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
282		if assignee == "" {
283			assignee = "ddx"
284		}
285		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
286		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
287			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
288		}
289		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
290		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
291			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
292		}
293		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
294		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
295			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
296		}
297		harness := rcfg.Harness()
298		model := rcfg.Model()
299		profile := rcfg.Profile()
300	
301		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
302		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
303	
304		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
305			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
306		}
307	
308		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
309			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
310			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
311			"harness":      harness,
312			"model":        model,
313			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
314			"assignee":     assignee,
315			"once":         runtime.Once,
316		})
317		defer func() {
318			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
319				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
320				"successes":           result.Successes,
321				"failures":            result.Failures,
322				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
323			})
324		}()
325	
326		for {
327			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
328			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
329			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
330			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
331			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
332				return result, err
333			}
334	
335			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, runtime.LabelFilter)
336			if err != nil {
337				return result, err
338			}
339			if !ok {
340				if result.Attempts == 0 {
341					result.NoReadyWork = true
342					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
343						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
344							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
345								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
346								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
347								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
348								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
349								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
350							}
351						}
352					}
353				}
354				if runtime.PollInterval <= 0 {
355					return result, nil
356				}
357				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, runtime.PollInterval); err != nil {
358					return result, err
359				}
360				continue
361			}
362	
363			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
364	
365			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
366			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
367			// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
368			if runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {
369				if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
370					if runtime.Log != nil {
371						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
372					}
373					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
374						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
375						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
376					})
377					continue
378				}
379			}
380	
381			// Routing preflight gate (FEAT-006 D3, ddx-98e6e9ef): consult the
382			// upstream typed-incompatibility surface BEFORE claiming. If the
383			// configured (harness, model) cannot serve the bead, exit the loop
384			// with a worker-level failure record — no claim, no executor
385			// invocation, no tier-attempt event burn.
386			if runtime.RoutePreflight != nil {
387				if rerr := runtime.RoutePreflight(ctx, harness, model); rerr != nil {
388					detail := fmt.Sprintf("routing preflight rejected (harness=%s model=%s): %s",
389						harness, model, rerr.Error())
390					if runtime.Log != nil {
391						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "routing preflight: %s (skipping %s, exiting loop)\n",
392							detail, candidate.ID)
393					}
394					emit("preflight.rejected", map[string]any{
395						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
396						"harness": harness,
397						"model":   model,
398						"reason":  rerr.Error(),
399					})
400					report := ExecuteBeadReport{
401						BeadID:  candidate.ID,
402						Status:  ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
403						Detail:  detail,
404						Harness: harness,
405						Model:   model,
406					}
407					result.Attempts++
408					result.Failures++
409					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
410					result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
411					return result, nil
412				}
413			}
414	
415			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
416				continue
417			}
418	
419			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
420				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
421				"title":    candidate.Title,
422				"assignee": assignee,
423			})
424	
425			if runtime.Log != nil {
426				if candidate.Title != "" {
427					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
428				} else {
429					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
430				}
431			}
432	
433			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
434			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
435			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
436			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
437			runStart := now()
438			phaseSeq := 0
439			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
440				phaseSeq++
441				emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
442					runtime.WorkerID, runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
443					harness, model, profile,
444					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
445				))
446			}
447	
448			nextPhase("queueing", false)
449	
450			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
451			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
452			hbWG.Add(1)
453			go func(beadID string) {
454				defer hbWG.Done()
455				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
456				defer ticker.Stop()
457				for {
458					select {
459					case <-hbCtx.Done():
460						return
461					case <-ticker.C:
462						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)
463					}
464				}
465			}(candidate.ID)
466	
467			nextPhase("running", false)
468	
469			report, err := w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)
470			hbCancel()
471			hbWG.Wait()
472			if err != nil {
473				report = ExecuteBeadReport{
474					BeadID: candidate.ID,
475					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
476					Detail: err.Error(),
477				}
478			}
479			if report.BeadID == "" {
480				report.BeadID = candidate.ID
481			}
482			if report.Status == "" {
483				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
484			}
485			if report.Detail == "" {
486				report.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(report.Status, "", "")
487			}
488	
489			result.Attempts++
490	
491			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
492				appendLoopRoutingEvidence(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, now().UTC())
493				// Close the bead early when review is skipped. The closure gate
494				// (ddx-e30e60a9) accepts this path because closing_commit_sha is
495				// set and there is no malformed-APPROVE event to reject.
496				reviewApproved := true
497				reviewSkipped := w.Reviewer == nil || runtime.NoReview || HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip")
498	
499				if reviewSkipped {
500					if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
501						return result, err
502					}
503				}
504	
505				if w.Reviewer != nil && !runtime.NoReview && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip") {
506					reviewRes, reviewErr := w.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev, report.Harness, report.Model)
507					if reviewErr != nil {
508						// FEAT-022 §12+§14: classify the failure into the four-class
509						// taxonomy and count prior review-error events scoped to the
510						// current result_rev. On the Nth failure emit a terminal
511						// review-manual-required event instead of yet another
512						// review-error, parking the bead so a subsequent loop
513						// iteration does NOT re-execute primary.
514						class := classifyReviewError(reviewErr, reviewRes)
515						prior := countPriorReviewErrors(w.Store, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev)
516						attemptCount := prior + 1
517						maxRetries := rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries()
518						if maxRetries <= 0 {
519							maxRetries = DefaultReviewMaxRetries
520						}
521						errSummary := EventBodySummary{
522							InputBytes:  0,
523							OutputBytes: 0,
524						}
525						if reviewRes != nil {
526							errSummary = EventBodySummary{
527								Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
528								Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
529								InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
530								OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
531								ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
532							}
533						}
534						if attemptCount >= maxRetries {
535							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
536							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
537								Kind:      "review-manual-required",
538								Summary:   class,
539								Body:      body,
540								Actor:     assignee,
541								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
542								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
543							})
544							// Park the bead with a long cooldown so subsequent
545							// loop iterations do NOT re-pick it for primary
546							// re-execution. Reviewer-failure invariant (§13) is
547							// preserved: the bead is NOT closed.
548							parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))
549							_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, "review-manual-required", class)
550						} else {
551							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
552							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
553								Kind:      "review-error",
554								Summary:   class,
555								Body:      body,
556								Actor:     assignee,
557								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
558								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
559							})
560						}
561					} else {
562						report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
563						report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
564						// Persist the full reviewer stream as an artifact so the
565						// event body never carries the raw stream (ddx-f8a11202).
566						// On error the artifact path is empty and the event body
567						// still contains the short verdict summary; callers of this
568						// loop can recover the full text from the reviewer session
569						// log if the artifact write failed.
570						artifactPath, artifactErr := persistReviewerStream(runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, report.AttemptID, reviewRes.RawOutput)
571						if artifactErr != nil && runtime.Log != nil {
572							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "reviewer stream artifact: %v\n", artifactErr)
573						}
574	
575						reviewSummary := EventBodySummary{
576							Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
577							Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
578							InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
579							OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
580							ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
581						}
582						switch reviewRes.Verdict {
583						case VerdictApprove:
584							// Approved: record the verdict event and then close.
585							// Closure must land AFTER the review event so the
586							// gate (ddx-e30e60a9) sees the terminal verdict.
587							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
588								Kind:      "review",
589								Summary:   "APPROVE",
590								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("APPROVE", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
591								Actor:     assignee,
592								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
593								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
594							})
595							if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); cerr != nil {
596								return result, cerr
597							}
598						case VerdictRequestChanges:
599							// Needs fixes: record the review verdict, then reopen
600							// with findings in notes. The review event must land
601							// even on non-approve paths so review-outcomes can
602							// attribute the rejection to the originating tier.
603							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
604								Kind:      "review",
605								Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
606								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
607								Actor:     assignee,
608								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
609								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
610							})
611							reopenNotes := reviewRes.Rationale
612							if reopenNotes == "" {
613								reopenNotes = reviewRes.RawOutput
614							}
615							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: REQUEST_CHANGES", reopenNotes); reopenErr != nil {
616								return result, reopenErr
617							}
618							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
619							report.Detail = "post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
620							reviewApproved = false
621						case VerdictBlock:
622							rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
623							if rationale == "" {
624								_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
625									Kind:      "review-malfunction",
626									Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
627									Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath), reviewSummary),
628									Actor:     assignee,
629									Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
630									CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
631								})
632								report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
633								report.Detail = "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
634								report.ReviewRationale = ""
635								reviewApproved = false
636								break
637							}
638							// Cannot proceed: record the verdict, then reopen and
639							// flag for human with BLOCK marker plus actionable rationale.
640							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
641								Kind:      "review",
642								Summary:   "BLOCK",
643								Body:      appendEventSummary(rationale, reviewSummary),
644								Actor:     assignee,
645								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
646								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
647							})
648							blockNotes := "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n" + rationale
649							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: BLOCK", blockNotes); reopenErr != nil {
650								return result, reopenErr
651							}
652							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
653							report.Detail = "post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)"
654							reviewApproved = false
655						}
656					}
657				}
658	
659				if reviewApproved {
660					result.Successes++
661					result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
662				} else {
663					result.Failures++
664					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
665				}
666			} else {
667				if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
668					return result, err
669				}
670				if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges {
671					count, cerr := w.Store.IncrNoChangesCount(candidate.ID)
672					if cerr != nil {
673						return result, cerr
674					}
675					satisfied, evidence, aerr := w.adjudicateNoChanges(ctx, candidate.ID, count, maxNoChangesBeforeClose, report.NoChangesRationale, candidate.Acceptance, runtime.ProjectRoot)
676					if aerr != nil {
677						return result, aerr
678					}
679					if satisfied {
680						// Adjudication confirmed bead is already satisfied.
681						// Set the terminal status BEFORE the close so the late
682						// executeBeadLoopEvent append captures "already_satisfied"
683						// (not "no_changes"), and emit an early execute-bead
684						// evidence event so the closure gate accepts even when
685						// BaseRev is empty (test fixtures and genuinely-no-commit
686						// satisfied beads).
687						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
688						if evidence != "" {
689							// Checker evidence explains why the bead is being closed;
690							// it takes precedence over the executor's attempt detail.
691							report.Detail = evidence
692						}
693						_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC()))
694						if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.BaseRev); cerr != nil {
695							return result, cerr
696						}
697						result.Successes++
698						result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
699					} else {
700						// Unresolved: suppress immediate retry so the queue can
701						// move on to other beads.
702						if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
703							retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(noProgressCooldown))
704							if cerr := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); cerr != nil {
705								return result, cerr
706							}
707							report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
708						}
709						result.Failures++
710						result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
711					}
712				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition {
713					// Executor declined the bead because it is too large to
714					// deliver in one pass. Record a structured
715					// `decomposition-recommendation` event and park the bead
716					// long enough that subsequent loop iterations do NOT
717					// re-attempt it. An operator (or helix-evolve) clears the
718					// cooldown — typically by splitting the bead.
719					body, mErr := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
720						"rationale":            report.DecompositionRationale,
721						"recommended_subbeads": report.DecompositionRecommendation,
722						"detail":               report.Detail,
723						"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
724						"session_id":           report.SessionID,
725					})
726					bodyStr := ""
727					if mErr == nil {
728						bodyStr = string(body)
729					} else {
730						bodyStr = fmt.Sprintf("rationale=%s\nrecommended_subbeads=%v",
731							report.DecompositionRationale, report.DecompositionRecommendation)
732					}
733					summary := "agent declined: needs decomposition"
734					if n := len(report.DecompositionRecommendation); n > 0 {
735						summary = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%d sub-beads)", summary, n)
736					}
737					_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
738						Kind:      "decomposition-recommendation",
739						Summary:   summary,
740						Body:      bodyStr,
741						Actor:     assignee,
742						Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
743						CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
744					})
745					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))
746					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
747						return result, err
748					}
749					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
750					result.Failures++
751					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
752				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed {
753					// Push failed after a successful local merge/ff. The bead is
754					// NOT closed (commits live only locally) — park it under the
755					// 24h cap so the loop re-attempts within a reasonable horizon.
756					// The push stderr in report.Detail is recorded as last_detail
757					// so it surfaces on the bead and on any direct claim attempt.
758					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))
759					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
760						return result, err
761					}
762					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
763					result.Failures++
764					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
765				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict {
766					// The local target ref was advanced and the loop's
767					// auto-pull/merge/retry recovery hit a merge conflict it
768					// cannot resolve without operator input. Record a
769					// `push-conflict` event with the conflict context so the
770					// operator can find and resolve it, and park the bead under
771					// the same 24h cap as push_failed.
772					body, mErr := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
773						"detail":     report.Detail,
774						"base_rev":   report.BaseRev,
775						"result_rev": report.ResultRev,
776						"session_id": report.SessionID,
777					})
778					bodyStr := report.Detail
779					if mErr == nil {
780						bodyStr = string(body)
781					}
782					_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
783						Kind:      "push-conflict",
784						Summary:   "auto-merge after push race could not be resolved",
785						Body:      bodyStr,
786						Actor:     assignee,
787						Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
788						CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
789					})
790					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))
791					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
792						return result, err
793					}
794					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
795					result.Failures++
796					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
797				} else {
798					if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
799						retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(noProgressCooldown))
800						if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
801							return result, err
802						}
803						report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
804					}
805					result.Failures++
806					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
807				}
808			}
809	
810			result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
811	
812			// Skip the late execute-bead append for already-satisfied beads —
813			// the satisfied path appends its own terminal event before
814			// CloseWithEvidence so the closure gate sees execution evidence.
815			// Duplicating it here would yield two identical events.
816			if report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
817				if err := w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC())); err != nil {
818					return result, err
819				}
820			}
821	
822			// Emit terminal progress phase event.
823			terminalPhase := "failed"
824			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
825				terminalPhase = "done"
826			} else if report.PreserveRef != "" {
827				terminalPhase = "preserved"
828			}
829			// Use the real attempt_id from the report if available.
830			finalAttemptID := report.AttemptID
831			if finalAttemptID == "" {
832				finalAttemptID = provAttemptID
833			}
834			phaseSeq++
835			emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
836				EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
837				AttemptID: finalAttemptID,
838				WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
839				ProjectID: runtime.ProjectRoot,
840				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
841				Harness:   harness,
842				Model:     model,
843				Profile:   profile,
844				Phase:     terminalPhase,
845				PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
846				Heartbeat: false,
847				TS:        now().UTC(),
848				ElapsedMS: now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
849				Message:   report.Detail,
850			})
851	
852			emit("bead.result", map[string]any{
853				"bead_id":              candidate.ID,
854				"status":               report.Status,
855				"detail":               report.Detail,
856				"session_id":           report.SessionID,
857				"result_rev":           report.ResultRev,
858				"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
859				"preserve_ref":         report.PreserveRef,
860				"no_changes_rationale": report.NoChangesRationale,
861				"duration_ms":          now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
862			})
863	
864			if runtime.Log != nil {
865				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "✓ %s → %s\n", candidate.ID, formatLoopResult(report))
866			}
867	
868			if runtime.Once {
869				return result, nil
870			}
871		}
872	}
873	
874	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) nextCandidate(attempted map[string]struct{}, labelFilter string) (bead.Bead, bool, error) {
875		ready, err := w.Store.ReadyExecution()
876		if err != nil {
877			return bead.Bead{}, false, err
878		}
879		for _, candidate := range ready {
880			if _, seen := attempted[candidate.ID]; seen {
881				continue
882			}
883			if labelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, labelFilter) {
884				continue
885			}
886			return candidate, true, nil
887		}
888		return bead.Bead{}, false, nil
889	}
890	
891	// appendLoopRoutingEvidence records a kind:routing evidence event on the bead
892	// from the executor's ExecuteBeadReport, so that review-outcomes analytics can
893	// attribute a subsequent review verdict to the originating provider/model tier.
894	// Best-effort: errors and missing-provider cases are silently ignored.
895	func appendLoopRoutingEvidence(store BeadEventAppender, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport, createdAt time.Time) {
896		if store == nil || beadID == "" {
897			return
898		}
899		provider := report.Provider
900		if provider == "" {
901			provider = report.Harness
902		}
903		if provider == "" {
904			return
905		}
906		body, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
907			"resolved_provider": provider,
908			"resolved_model":    report.Model,
909			"fallback_chain":    []string{},
910			"requested_profile": report.RequestedProfile,
911			"requested_tier":    report.RequestedTier,
912			"resolved_tier":     report.ResolvedTier,
913			"escalation_count":  report.EscalationCount,
914			"final_tier":        report.FinalTier,
915		})
916		if err != nil {
917			return
918		}
919		summary := "provider=" + provider
920		if report.Model != "" {
921			summary += " model=" + report.Model
922		}
923		_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
924			Kind:      "routing",
925			Summary:   summary,
926			Body:      string(body),
927			Actor:     "ddx",
928			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
929			CreatedAt: createdAt,
930		})
931	}
932	
933	func executeBeadLoopEvent(report ExecuteBeadReport, actor string, createdAt time.Time) bead.BeadEvent {
934		parts := []string{}
935		if report.Detail != "" {
936			parts = append(parts, report.Detail)
937		}
938		if report.Tier != "" {
939			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s", report.Tier))
940		}
941		if report.ProbeResult != "" {
942			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("probe_result=%s", report.ProbeResult))
943		}
944		if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
945			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("rationale: %s", report.NoChangesRationale))
946		}
947		if report.ReviewRationale != "" {
948			parts = append(parts, report.ReviewRationale)
949		}
950		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
951			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("preserve_ref=%s", report.PreserveRef))
952		}
953		if report.ResultRev != "" {
954			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("result_rev=%s", report.ResultRev))
955		}
956		if report.BaseRev != "" {
957			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("base_rev=%s", report.BaseRev))
958		}
959		if report.RetryAfter != "" {
960			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("retry_after=%s", report.RetryAfter))
961		}
962	
963		return bead.BeadEvent{
964			Kind:      "execute-bead",
965			Summary:   report.Status,
966			Body:      strings.Join(parts, "\n"),
967			Actor:     actor,
968			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
969			CreatedAt: createdAt,
970		}
971	}
972	
973	// writeLoopEvent emits one structured JSONL line to sink describing a
974	// milestone in an execute-bead loop run. Entries use the same envelope as
975	// the ddx-agent harness (session_id/seq/type/ts/data) so existing log
976	// aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, ddx server workers log) can parse
977	// the stream uniformly. Errors are swallowed: structured logging must
978	// never break the core execute-loop.
979	func writeLoopEvent(sink io.Writer, sessionID, eventType string, data map[string]any, ts time.Time) {
980		if sink == nil {
981			return
982		}
983		if data == nil {
984			data = map[string]any{}
985		}
986		entry := map[string]any{
987			"session_id": sessionID,
988			"type":       eventType,
989			"ts":         ts.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
990			"data":       data,
991		}
992		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
993		if err != nil {
994			return
995		}
996		_, _ = sink.Write(line)
997		_, _ = sink.Write([]byte("\n"))
998	}
999	
1000	// formatLoopResult returns a concise human-readable summary of a bead execution
1001	// result using merged/preserved/error terminology instead of raw status codes.
1002	func formatLoopResult(report ExecuteBeadReport) string {
1003		switch report.Status {
1004		case ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
1005			shortRev := report.ResultRev
1006			if len(shortRev) > 8 {
1007				shortRev = shortRev[:8]
1008			}
1009			if shortRev != "" {
1010				return fmt.Sprintf("merged (%s)", shortRev)
1011			}
1012			return "merged"
1013		case ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
1014			return "already_satisfied"
1015		case ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
1016			if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
1017				return fmt.Sprintf("no_changes: %s", report.NoChangesRationale)
1018			}
1019			return "no_changes"
1020		default:
1021			detail := report.Detail
1022			if detail == "" {
1023				detail = report.Status
1024			}
1025			if report.PreserveRef != "" {
1026				return fmt.Sprintf("preserved: %s", detail)
1027			}
1028			return fmt.Sprintf("error: %s", detail)
1029		}
1030	}
1031	
1032	// reCommitSHA matches a 7-to-40 character lowercase hex string that looks like a
1033	// git commit SHA. Used to detect whether a no_changes rationale cites a prior commit.
1034	var reCommitSHA = regexp.MustCompile(`\b[0-9a-f]{7,40}\b`)
1035	
1036	// reTestFuncName matches a Go test function name (TestXxx or BenchmarkXxx).
1037	var reTestFuncName = regexp.MustCompile(`\b(?:Test|Benchmark)[A-Z]\w*\b`)
1038	
1039	// rationaleIsSpecific returns true when the rationale string contains a reference
1040	// specific enough to treat a no_changes outcome as already_satisfied on the first
1041	// attempt. Currently this means: the rationale cites a commit SHA (7+ hex chars)
1042	// or a Go test function name. Vague rationales ("nothing to do") return false.
1043	func rationaleIsSpecific(rationale string) bool {
1044		if rationale == "" {
1045			return false
1046		}
1047		return reCommitSHA.MatchString(rationale) || reTestFuncName.MatchString(rationale)
1048	}
1049	
1050	// adjudicateNoChanges runs the no-change adjudication step for a bead.
1051	// It returns (satisfied, evidence, err). When satisfied is true the bead
1052	// should be closed as already_satisfied with the evidence string. When false
1053	// retry suppression (cooldown) should be applied and the bead left open.
1054	//
1055	// If a SatisfactionChecker is configured it is called first. Otherwise:
1056	//   - When the report carries a specific rationale (cites a commit SHA or test
1057	//     name), the bead is closed as already_satisfied on the first occurrence.
1058	//   - Otherwise the default count-based rule applies (close after maxNoChangesBeforeClose).
1059	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) adjudicateNoChanges(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount, maxNoChangesBeforeClose int, rationale, acceptance, projectRoot string) (bool, string, error) {
1060		if w.SatisfactionChecker != nil {
1061			return w.SatisfactionChecker.CheckSatisfied(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
1062		}
1063		candidate := false
1064		evidence := ""
1065		switch {
1066		case rationaleIsSpecific(rationale):
1067			candidate = true
1068			evidence = rationale
1069		case noChangesCount >= maxNoChangesBeforeClose:
1070			candidate = true
1071			evidence = fmt.Sprintf("no_changes on %d consecutive attempt(s); bead treated as already satisfied", noChangesCount)
1072		}
1073		if !candidate {
1074			return false, "", nil
1075		}
1076		// Tighten the gate: when AC names structural properties (test functions,
1077		// deleted files, removed struct fields), refuse already_satisfied unless
1078		// each property holds in the worktree / rationale. Prevents false closes
1079		// where a regression suite passes but the AC's specific contract is unmet.
1080		if claims := ParseACClaims(acceptance); len(claims) > 0 {
1081			if ok, why := VerifyACClaims(claims, projectRoot, rationale); !ok {
1082				return false, why, nil
1083			}
1084		}
1085		return true, evidence, nil
1086	}
1087	
1088	func shouldSuppressNoProgress(report ExecuteBeadReport) bool {
1089		if report.BaseRev == "" || report.ResultRev == "" {
1090			return false
1091		}
1092		return report.BaseRev == report.ResultRev
1093	}
1094	
1095	// classifyReviewError maps a reviewer error and partial result into one of the
1096	// four FEAT-022 §12 taxonomy classes. Resolution order:
1097	//  1. reviewRes.Error if it's already one of the canonical class identifiers
1098	//     (the reviewer set it explicitly — preferred path).
1099	//  2. The error string text (for backwards-compatible reviewers that only
1100	//     embed the class in their message).
1101	//  3. ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a fallback when the strict-parse error
1102	//     leaks through a reviewer that did not set Error.
1103	//  4. Default to transport (network/provider-side failure) when nothing else
1104	//     matches.
1105	func classifyReviewError(reviewErr error, reviewRes *ReviewResult) string {
1106		if reviewRes != nil {
1107			switch reviewRes.Error {
1108			case evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1109				evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
1110				evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
1111				evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport:
1112				return reviewRes.Error
1113			}
1114		}
1115		msg := ""
1116		if reviewErr != nil {
1117			msg = reviewErr.Error()
1118		}
1119		switch {
1120		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow):
1121			return evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow
1122		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty):
1123			return evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
1124		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable):
1125			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1126		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport):
1127			return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1128		case reviewErr != nil && strings.Contains(msg, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable.Error()):
1129			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1130		}
1131		return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1132	}
1133	
1134	// reResultRevField extracts the result_rev value from a structured review-error
1135	// event body (one `result_rev=<sha>` line per event, written by
1136	// reviewErrorEventBody). The format is intentionally line-oriented so it
1137	// survives the AppendEvent body cap without losing the rev association.
1138	var reResultRevField = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^result_rev=([^\s]+)\s*$`)
1139	
1140	// countPriorReviewErrors returns the number of `review-error` events already
1141	// recorded against this bead whose body cites the given result_rev. This is
1142	// the FEAT-022 §14 retry counter — it is event-scoped (no separate counter
1143	// field on the bead) so a fresh result_rev naturally resets the count.
1144	func countPriorReviewErrors(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, resultRev string) int {
1145		if resultRev == "" {
1146			return 0
1147		}
1148		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
1149		if err != nil {
1150			return 0
1151		}
1152		n := 0
1153		for _, ev := range events {
1154			if ev.Kind != "review-error" {
1155				continue
1156			}
1157			m := reResultRevField.FindStringSubmatch(ev.Body)
1158			if m == nil {
1159				continue
1160			}
1161			if m[1] == resultRev {
1162				n++
1163			}
1164		}
1165		return n
1166	}
1167	
1168	// reviewErrorEventBody is the canonical body shape for review-error and
1169	// review-manual-required events. It carries the failure class, attempt count,
1170	// and result_rev as discrete lines so operators can grep without parsing the
1171	// full reviewer error text. The trailing free-form message is the raw
1172	// reviewer-error string for forensics.
1173	func reviewErrorEventBody(class string, attemptCount int, resultRev, message string) string {
1174		var b strings.Builder
1175		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "failure_class=%s\n", class)
1176		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "attempt_count=%d\n", attemptCount)
1177		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
1178		if message != "" {
1179			b.WriteString("\n")
1180			b.WriteString(message)
1181		}
1182		return b.String()
1183	}
1184	
1185	func sleepWithContext(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration) error {
1186		if d <= 0 {
1187			return nil
1188		}
1189		timer := time.NewTimer(d)
1190		defer timer.Stop()
1191		select {
1192		case <-ctx.Done():
1193			return ctx.Err()
1194		case <-timer.C:
1195			return nil
1196		}
1197	}
1198
